{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_4bbe99d58b444b89b12ce0ed","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Test Patterns Guide\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tThis guide explains the patterns and best practices for adding new error test cases to the ARMOR test suite. It provides step-by-step instructions for common scenarios and helps you choose the right approach for your testing needs.\n6\t\n7\t## Choosing Your Testing Approach\n8\t\n9\tBefore writing a test, decide which approach best fits your scenario:\n10\t\n11\t### When to Use **Fixtures**\n12\t- Testing standard HTTP errors (404, 500, etc.)\n13\t- Need reusable error responses\n14\t- Want consistent error structures across tests\n15\t- Testing against pre-defined error scenarios\n16\t\n17\t### When to Use **Validation Helpers**\n18\t- Testing custom API responses\n19\t- Need fine-grained control over validation\n20\t- Testing live endpoints\n21\t- Want to validate specific fields or patterns\n22\t\n23\t### When to Use **Test Tables**\n24\t- Testing multiple similar scenarios\n25\t- Data-driven testing with variations\n26\t- Need to test the same logic with different inputs\n27\t- Want organized, maintainable test cases\n28\t\n29\t## Pattern 1: Testing Standard Error Responses\n30\t\n31\t### Scenario: Testing a 404 Not Found Error\n32\t\n33\tUse this pattern when testing standard HTTP errors.\n34\t\n35\t#### Step-by-Step Guide\n36\t\n37\t**1. Import the necessary fixtures and helpers:**\n38\t\n39\t```python\n40\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import not_found_fixture\n41\tfrom tests.test_helpers import validate_http_status, validate_json_content_type\n42\tfrom tests.test_error_response_validation import validate_standard_error_response\n43\t```\n44\t\n45\t**2. Create the fixture with your test data:**\n46\t\n47\t```python\n48\tdef test_user_not_found():\n49\t    \"\"\"Test 404 response when user doesn't exist.\"\"\"\n50\t    # Create a 404 fixture for the specific endpoint\n51\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n52\t```\n53\t\n54\t**3. Validate the response:**\n55\t\n56\t```python\n57\t    # Validate status code\n58\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n59\t    \n60\t    # Validate content type\n61\t    validate_json_content_type(fixture.to_tuple())\n62\t    \n63\t    # Validate error structure\n64\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n65\t    \n66\t    # Validate specific fields\n67\t    assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n68\t    assert 'not found' in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n69\t```\n70\t\n71\t**4. Run the test:**\n72\t\n73\t```bash\n74\tpytest tests/test_my_errors.py::test_user_not_found -v\n75\t```\n76\t\n77\t### Complete Example\n78\t\n79\t```python\n80\tdef test_user_not_found():\n81\t    \"\"\"Test 404 Not Found response for non-existent user.\"\"\"\n82\t    # Create fixture\n83\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n84\t    \n85\t    # Convert to tuple for validation\n86\t    response = fixture.to_tuple()\n87\t    \n88\t    # Validate status code\n89\t    validate_http_status(response, 404)\n90\t    \n91\t    # Validate content type\n92\t    validate_json_content_type(response)\n93\t    \n94\t    # Validate error structure\n95\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n96\t    \n97\t    # Validate specific fields\n98\t    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'not_found'\n99\t    assert fixture.body['code'] == 404\n100\t    assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n101\t    assert 'user' in fixture.body['message'].lower() or '999' in fixture.body['message']\n102\t```\n103\t\n104\t## Pattern 2: Testing Custom Error Responses\n105\t\n106\t### Scenario: Testing a Custom Validation Error\n107\t\n108\tUse this pattern when you need to test errors specific to your API.\n109\t\n110\t#### Step-by-Step Guide\n111\t\n112\t**1. Import the create_error_response builder:**\n113\t\n114\t```python\n115\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import create_error_response\n116\tfrom tests.test_helpers import validate_http_status\n117\t```\n118\t\n119\t**2. Define your custom error structure:**\n120\t\n121\t```python\n122\tdef test_email_validation_error():\n123\t    \"\"\"Test custom email validation error.\"\"\"\n124\t    # Create a custom error response\n125\t    fixture = create_error_response(\n126\t        status=400,\n127\t        error_type=\"validation_error\",\n128\t        message=\"Email address is invalid\",\n129\t        code=400,\n130\t        details={\n131\t            'field': 'email',\n132\t            'value': 'invalid-email',\n133\t            'error': 'Invalid email format'\n134\t        }\n135\t    )\n136\t```\n137\t\n138\t**3. Validate your custom error:**\n139\t\n140\t```python\n141\t    # Validate status code\n142\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 400)\n143\t    \n144\t    # Validate custom fields\n145\t    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'validation_error'\n146\t    assert fixture.body['details']['field'] == 'email'\n147\t    assert 'invalid' in fixture.body['details']['value']\n148\t```\n149\t\n150\t### Complete Example\n151\t\n152\t```python\n153\tdef test_email_validation_error():\n154\t    \"\"\"Test validation error for invalid email format.\"\"\"\n155\t    # Create custom validation error\n156\t    fixture = create_error_response(\n157\t        status=400,\n158\t        error_type=\"validation_error\",\n159\t        message=\"Email address is invalid\",\n160\t        code=400,\n161\t        details={\n162\t            'field': 'email',\n163\t            'provided_value': 'not-an-email',\n164\t            'constraint': 'must be valid email format',\n165\t            'example': 'user@example.com'\n166\t        }\n167\t    )\n168\t    \n169\t    # Basic validation\n170\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 400)\n171\t    \n172\t    # Custom field validation\n173\t    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'validation_error'\n174\t    assert 'email' in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n175\t    assert fixture.body['details']['field'] == 'email'\n176\t    assert '@' not in fixture.body['details']['provided_value']\n177\t    assert 'example.com' in fixture.body['details']['example']\n178\t```\n179\t\n180\t## Pattern 3: Testing Multiple Similar Scenarios\n181\t\n182\t### Scenario: Testing 404 Errors Across Multiple Endpoints\n183\t\n184\tUse this pattern when you have multiple similar test cases.\n185\t\n186\t#### Step-by-Step Guide\n187\t\n188\t**1. Import test table components:**\n189\t\n190\t```python\n191\tfrom tests.test_tables import TestCase, create_not_found_test_table, run_test_table\n192\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import not_found_fixture\n193\t```\n194\t\n195\t**2. Define your test cases:**\n196\t\n197\t```python\n198\tdef test_multiple_404_scenarios():\n199\t    \"\"\"Test 404 responses across different endpoints.\"\"\"\n200\t    # Define test cases\n201\t    test_cases = [\n202\t        TestCase(\n203\t            name=\"User not found\",\n204\t            path=\"/api/users/999\",\n205\t            expected_code=\"not_found\"\n206\t        ),\n207\t        TestCase(\n208\t            name=\"Item not found\",\n209\t            path=\"/api/items/abc\",\n210\t            expected_code=\"not_found\"\n211\t        ),\n212\t        TestCase(\n213\t            name=\"Order not found\",\n214\t            path=\"/api/orders/xyz\",\n215\t            expected_code=\"not_found\"\n216\t        ),\n217\t    ]\n218\t```\n219\t\n220\t**3. Create and run the test table:**\n221\t\n222\t```python\n223\t    # Create test table\n224\t    table = create_not_found_test_table(test_cases)\n225\t    \n226\t    # Run all test cases\n227\t    results = []\n228\t    for case in test_cases:\n229\t        # Create fixture for each case\n230\t        fixture = not_found_fixture(path=case.path)\n231\t        \n232\t        # Validate\n233\t        validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n234\t        validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n235\t        \n236\t        results.append(True)\n237\t    \n238\t    # Assert all passed\n239\t    assert all(results)\n240\t```\n241\t\n242\t### Complete Example\n243\t\n244\t```python\n245\tdef test_multiple_not_found_scenarios():\n246\t    \"\"\"Test 404 Not Found for various resource types.\"\"\"\n247\t    # Define test cases\n248\t    scenarios = [\n249\t        (\"/api/users/999\", \"user\"),\n250\t        (\"/api/items/abc\", \"item\"),\n251\t        (\"/api/orders/xyz\", \"order\"),\n252\t        (\"/api/products/invalid\", \"product\"),\n253\t    ]\n254\t    \n255\t    # Test each scenario\n256\t    for path, resource_type in scenarios:\n257\t        # Create fixture\n258\t        fixture = not_found_fixture(path=path)\n259\t        \n260\t        # Validate structure\n261\t        validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n262\t        validate_json_content_type(fixture.to_tuple())\n263\t        validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n264\t        \n265\t        # Validate path is in error details\n266\t        assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == path\n267\t        \n268\t        # Validate resource type is mentioned (optional)\n269\t        # assert resource_type in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n270\t```\n271\t\n272\t## Pattern 4: Testing Live Endpoints\n273\t\n274\t### Scenario: Testing a Real API Endpoint\n275\t\n276\tUse this pattern when testing against a running server.\n277\t\n278\t#### Step-by-Step Guide\n279\t\n280\t**1. Import HTTP request helper:**\n281\t\n282\t```python\n283\tfrom tests.test_helpers import make_http_request, validate_http_status\n284\t```\n285\t\n286\t**2. Make the HTTP request:**\n287\t\n288\t```python\n289\tdef test_live_endpoint():\n290\t    \"\"\"Test a live API endpoint.\"\"\"\n291\t    # Make request to test server\n292\t    response = make_http_request(\n293\t        base_url='http://localhost:8080',\n294\t        path='/api/users/999',\n295\t        method='GET'\n296\t    )\n297\t```\n298\t\n299\t**3. Validate the response:**\n300\t\n301\t```python\n302\t    # Check if request succeeded\n303\t    if not response['success']:\n304\t        pytest.skip(f\"Server not available: {response['error']}\")\n305\t    \n306\t    # Validate status code\n307\t    validate_http_status(response['status_code'], 404)\n308\t    \n309\t    # Parse and validate body\n310\t    import json\n311\t    body = json.loads(response['body'])\n312\t    validate_standard_error_response(body)\n313\t```\n314\t\n315\t### Complete Example\n316\t\n317\t```python\n318\t@pytest.mark.integration\n319\tdef test_live_user_endpoint():\n320\t    \"\"\"Test live user endpoint returns proper error for non-existent user.\"\"\"\n321\t    # Make request\n322\t    response = make_http_request(\n323\t        base_url='http://localhost:8080',\n324\t        path='/api/users/999',\n325\t        method='GET',\n326\t        timeout=5\n327\t    )\n328\t    \n329\t    # Skip if server not available\n330\t    if not response['success']:\n331\t        pytest.skip(f\"Server not available: {response['error']}\")\n332\t    \n333\t    # Validate response\n334\t    validate_http_status(response['status_code'], 404)\n335\t    validate_json_content_type((response['status_code'], response['headers'], ''))\n336\t    \n337\t    # Parse and validate body\n338\t    import json\n339\t    body = json.loads(response['body'])\n340\t    validate_standard_error_response(body)\n341\t    \n342\t    # Validate specific fields\n343\t    assert body['error'] == 'not_found'\n344\t    assert body['code'] == 404\n345\t    assert body['details']['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n346\t```\n347\t\n348\t## Pattern 5: Testing HTTP Methods\n349\t\n350\t### Scenario: Testing Method Not Allowed Errors\n351\t\n352\tUse this pattern when testing HTTP method restrictions.\n353\t\n354\t#### Step-by-Step Guide\n355\t\n356\t```python\n357\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import method_not_allowed_fixture\n358\t\n359\tdef test_method_not_allowed():\n360\t    \"\"\"Test 405 Method Not Allowed for DELETE on read-only endpoint.\"\"\"\n361\t    # Create 405 fixture\n362\t    fixture = method_not_allowed_fixture(\n363\t        path=\"/api/stats\",\n364\t        method=\"DELETE\",\n365\t        allowed_methods=[\"GET\", \"HEAD\", \"OPTIONS\"]\n366\t    )\n367\t    \n368\t    # Validate status code\n369\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 405)\n370\t    \n371\t    # Validate Allow header is present\n372\t    assert 'Allow' in fixture.headers\n373\t    assert 'GET' in fixture.headers['Allow']\n374\t    \n375\t    # Validate error body\n376\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n377\t    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'method_not_allowed'\n378\t    assert fixture.body['details']['method'] == 'DELETE'\n379\t```\n380\t\n381\t## Pattern 6: Testing Content-Type Errors\n382\t\n383\t### Scenario: Testing Unsupported Media Type Errors\n384\t\n385\tUse this pattern when testing content-type validation.\n386\t\n387\t```python\n388\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import unsupported_media_type_fixture\n389\t\n390\tdef test_unsupported_media_type():\n391\t    \"\"\"Test 415 Unsupported Media Type for XML request to JSON-only endpoint.\"\"\"\n392\t    # Create 415 fixture\n393\t    fixture = unsupported_media_type_fixture(\n394\t        path=\"/api/users\",\n395\t        content_type=\"application/xml\",\n396\t        supported_types=[\"application/json\"]\n397\t    )\n398\t    \n399\t    # Validate status code\n400\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 415)\n401\t    \n402\t    # Validate error details\n403\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n404\t    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'unsupported_media_type'\n405\t    assert fixture.body['details']['provided_type'] == 'application/xml'\n406\t    assert fixture.body['details']['supported_types'] == ['application/json']\n407\t```\n408\t\n409\t## Pattern 7: Testing Server Errors\n410\t\n411\t### Scenario: Testing Internal Server Error Responses\n412\t\n413\tUse this pattern when testing server error handling.\n414\t\n415\t```python\n416\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import internal_server_error_fixture\n417\t\n418\tdef test_internal_server_error():\n419\t    \"\"\"Test 500 Internal Server Error response.\"\"\"\n420\t    # Create 500 fixture\n421\t    fixture = internal_server_error_fixture(\n422\t        path=\"/api/users\",\n423\t        error_id=\"ERR-DB-001\"\n424\t    )\n425\t    \n426\t    # Validate status code\n427\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 500)\n428\t    \n429\t    # Validate error structure\n430\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n431\t    \n432\t    # Validate server error specifics\n433\t    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'internal_server_error'\n434\t    assert 'error_id' in fixture.body['details']\n435\t    assert fixture.headers.get('X-Error-ID') == 'ERR-DB-001'\n436\t```\n437\t\n438\t## Pattern 8: Testing CORS Headers\n439\t\n440\t### Scenario: Testing CORS Configuration\n441\t\n442\tUse this pattern when testing CORS headers.\n443\t\n444\t```python\n445\tfrom tests.test_helpers import validate_cors_headers\n446\t\n447\tdef test_cors_headers():\n448\t    \"\"\"Test CORS headers are properly configured.\"\"\"\n449\t    # Create response with CORS headers\n450\t    response = (\n451\t        200,\n452\t        {\n453\t            'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': 'https://example.com',\n454\t            'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',\n455\t            'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials': 'true'\n456\t        },\n457\t        '{}'\n458\t    )\n459\t    \n460\t    # Validate CORS headers\n461\t    validate_cors_headers(\n462\t        response,\n463\t        expected_origin='https://example.com'\n464\t    )\n465\t```\n466\t\n467\t## Pattern 9: Testing Authentication Errors\n468\t\n469\t### Scenario: Testing Authentication/Authorization Errors\n470\t\n471\tUse this pattern when testing auth failures.\n472\t\n473\t```python\n474\tfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import create_error_response\n475\t\n476\tdef test_authentication_error():\n477\t    \"\"\"Test 401 Unauthorized response for missing credentials.\"\"\"\n478\t    # Create 401 fixture\n479\t    fixture = create_error_response(\n480\t        status=401,\n481\t        error_type=\"unauthorized\",\n482\t        message=\"Authentication required\",\n483\t        code=401,\n484\t        details={\n485\t            'auth_type': 'Bearer',\n486\t            'realm': 'api'\n487\t        }\n488\t    )\n489\t    \n490\t    # Validate\n491\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 401)\n492\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n493\t    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'unauthorized'\n494\t```\n495\t\n496\t## Pattern 10: Batch Testing Multiple Fixtures\n497\t\n498\t### Scenario: Testing All Fixtures in a Category\n499\t\n500\tUse this pattern to validate all fixtures of a certain type.\n501\t\n502\t```python\n503\tdef test_all_client_error_fixtures():\n504\t    \"\"\"Test all client error fixtures are valid.\"\"\"\n505\t    # Get all client error fixtures\n506\t    from tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import CLIENT_ERROR_FIXTURES\n507\t    \n508\t    # Test each fixture\n509\t    for name, fixture in CLIENT_ERROR_FIXTURES.items():\n510\t        # Validate status code is in client error range\n511\t        assert 400 <= fixture.status_code < 500, \\\n512\t            f\"{name} has invalid status code {fixture.status_code}\"\n513\t        \n514\t        # Validate error structure\n515\t        validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n516\t        \n517\t        print(f\"✓ {name} validated\")\n518\t```\n519\t\n520\t## Best Practices\n521\t\n522\t### 1. Test Structure\n523\t\n524\tOrganize your tests logically:\n525\t\n526\t```python\n527\tclass TestUserErrors:\n528\t    \"\"\"Tests for user-related error responses.\"\"\"\n529\t    \n530\t    def test_user_not_found(self):\n531\t        \"\"\"Test 404 for non-existent user.\"\"\"\n532\t        pass\n533\t    \n534\t    def test_user_validation_error(self):\n535\t        \"\"\"Test 400 for invalid user data.\"\"\"\n536\t        pass\n537\t    \n538\t    def test_user_unauthorized(self):\n539\t        \"\"\"Test 401 for unauthorized access.\"\"\"\n540\t        pass\n541\t```\n542\t\n543\t### 2. Descriptive Test Names\n544\t\n545\tUse clear, descriptive test names:\n546\t\n547\t```python\n548\t# Good\n549\tdef test_user_not_found_returns_404_with_path_in_details():\n550\t    \"\"\"Test that requesting non-existent user returns 404 with path in error details.\"\"\"\n551\t    pass\n552\t\n553\t# Less clear\n554\tdef test_error():\n555\t    pass\n556\t```\n557\t\n558\t### 3. Validation Order\n559\t\n560\tValidate in a logical order:\n561\t\n562\t```python\n563\tdef test_validation_order():\n564\t    \"\"\"Demonstrate proper validation order.\"\"\"\n565\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test\")\n566\t    response = fixture.to_tuple()\n567\t    \n568\t    # 1. Validate status code (fastest check)\n569\t    validate_http_status(response, 404)\n570\t    \n571\t    # 2. Validate content type (medium check)\n572\t    validate_json_content_type(response)\n573\t    \n574\t    # 3. Validate error structure (slower check)\n575\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n576\t    \n577\t    # 4. Validate specific fields (most specific)\n578\t    assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == '/api/test'\n579\t```\n580\t\n581\t### 4. Error Messages\n582\t\n583\tUse assertion messages for clarity:\n584\t\n585\t```python\n586\tdef test_with_clear_messages():\n587\t    \"\"\"Test with clear assertion messages.\"\"\"\n588\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test\")\n589\t    \n590\t    # Clear assertion messages\n591\t    assert fixture.status_code == 404, \\\n592\t        f\"Expected 404, got {fixture.status_code}\"\n593\t    \n594\t    assert 'path' in fixture.body['details'], \\\n595\t        \"Error details should include 'path' field\"\n596\t```\n597\t\n598\t### 5. Test Independence\n599\t\n600\tEnsure tests don't depend on each other:\n601\t\n602\t```python\n603\t# Good - each test is independent\n604\tdef test_scenario_1():\n605\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test1\")\n606\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n607\t\n608\tdef test_scenario_2():\n609\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test2\")\n610\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n611\t\n612\t# Bad - test_scenario_2 depends on test_scenario_1\n613\tshared_fixture = None\n614\t\n615\tdef test_scenario_1():\n616\t    global shared_fixture\n617\t    shared_fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test\")\n618\t\n619\tdef test_scenario_2():\n620\t    validate_http_status(shared_fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n621\t```\n622\t\n623\t## Common Testing Scenarios\n624\t\n625\t### Scenario 1: Testing a New Endpoint\n626\t\n627\t```python\n628\tdef test_new_endpoint():\n629\t    \"\"\"Test a new endpoint returns proper error for invalid input.\"\"\"\n630\t    # Create fixture for your endpoint\n631\t    fixture = create_error_response(\n632\t        status=400,\n633\t        error_type=\"invalid_input\",\n634\t        message=\"Invalid input parameter\",\n635\t        code=400,\n636\t        details={'parameter': 'sort', 'value': 'invalid'}\n637\t    )\n638\t    \n639\t    # Validate\n640\t    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 400)\n641\t    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n642\t```\n643\t\n644\t### Scenario 2: Testing Error Messages\n645\t\n646\t```python\n647\tdef test_error_message_content():\n648\t    \"\"\"Test that error messages are user-friendly.\"\"\"\n649\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n650\t    \n651\t    # Check message contains helpful information\n652\t    assert 'users/999' in fixture.body['message'] or \\\n653\t           'user' in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n654\t    \n655\t    # Check message is not too technical\n656\t    assert 'exception' not in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n657\t    assert 'traceback' not in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n658\t```\n659\t\n660\t### Scenario 3: Testing Error Details\n661\t\n662\t```python\n663\tdef test_error_details_completeness():\n664\t    \"\"\"Test that error details include relevant information.\"\"\"\n665\t    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n666\t    \n667\t    # Check required detail fields\n668\t    details = fixture.body['details']\n669\t    assert 'path' in details\n670\t    assert details['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n671\t```\n672\t\n673\t## Running Tests\n674\t\n675\t### Run All Tests\n676\t\n677\t```bash\n678\tpytest tests/ -v\n679\t```\n680\t\n681\t### Run Specific Test File\n682\t\n683\t```bash\n684\tpytest tests/test_my_errors.py -v\n685\t```\n686\t\n687\t### Run Specific Test\n688\t\n689\t```bash\n690\tpytest tests/test_my_errors.py::test_user_not_found -v\n691\t```\n692\t\n693\t### Run with Coverage\n694\t\n695\t```bash\n696\tpytest tests/ --cov=tests --cov-report=html\n697\t```\n698\t\n699\t## Troubleshooting\n700\t\n701\t### Common Issues\n702\t\n703\t**1. Import Errors**\n704\t\n705\t```bash\n706\t# Error: ImportError: No module named 'tests.fixtures'\n707\t# Solution: Ensure you're running from the project root\n708\tcd /home/coding/ARMOR\n709\tpytest tests/\n710\t```\n711\t\n712\t**2. Fixture Not Found**\n713\t\n714\t```python\n715\t# Error: get_fixture() returns None\n716\t# Solution: Check fixture name spelling and availability\n717\tnames = list_fixtures()\n718\tprint(names)  # Check if your fixture exists\n719\t```\n720\t\n721\t**3. Validation Failures**\n722\t\n723\t```python\n724\t# Error: StatusValidationError\n725\t# Solution: Check your expected vs actual status codes\n726\ttry:\n727\t    validate_http_status(response, 200)\n728\texcept StatusValidationError as e:\n729\t    print(f\"Expected 200, got {e.actual}\")\n730\t```\n731\t\n732\t## Adding New Fixtures\n733\t\n734\tIf you need a new fixture type:\n735\t\n736\t1. **Add to `fixtures/error_scenarios.py`:**\n737\t\n738\t```python\n739\tdef conflict_fixture(path: str, message: str = None) -> ErrorResponseFixture:\n740\t    \"\"\"Create a 409 Conflict error fixture.\"\"\"\n741\t    return create_error_response(\n742\t        status=409,\n743\t        error_type=\"conflict\",\n744\t        message=message or f\"Resource conflict at {path}\",\n745\t        code=409,\n746\t        details={'path': path, 'conflict_type': 'resource_exists'}\n747\t    )\n748\t```\n749\t\n750\t2. **Export from `__init__.py`:**\n751\t\n752\t```python\n753\tfrom .error_scenarios import conflict_fixture\n754\t```\n755\t\n756\t3. **Add to collection (if applicable):**\n757\t\n758\t```python\n759\tALL_ERROR_FIXTURES['409_conflict'] = conflict_fixture()\n760\t```\n761\t\n762\t## Next Steps\n763\t\n764\t- Review [README.md](README.md) for infrastructure overview\n765\t- Check [example_test.py](example_test.py) for runnable examples\n766\t- See [fixtures/README.md](fixtures/README.md) for fixture documentation\n767\t- Reference [TEST_TABLE_GUIDE.md](TEST_TABLE_GUIDE.md) for test table usage\n768\t\n769\tBead: bf-qxhx20\n770\tCreated: 2026-07-15\n771\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5c219981-7007-4ac8-9382-afcc0a8100f4","uuid":"07dcafb1-7980-40ba-a4fc-19d9b3449978","timestamp":"2026-07-15T17:59:43.065Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/tests/TEST_PATTERNS.md","content":"# Test Patterns Guide\n\n## Overview\n\nThis guide explains the patterns and best practices for adding new error test cases to the ARMOR test suite. It provides step-by-step instructions for common scenarios and helps you choose the right approach for your testing needs.\n\n## Choosing Your Testing Approach\n\nBefore writing a test, decide which approach best fits your scenario:\n\n### When to Use **Fixtures**\n- Testing standard HTTP errors (404, 500, etc.)\n- Need reusable error responses\n- Want consistent error structures across tests\n- Testing against pre-defined error scenarios\n\n### When to Use **Validation Helpers**\n- Testing custom API responses\n- Need fine-grained control over validation\n- Testing live endpoints\n- Want to validate specific fields or patterns\n\n### When to Use **Test Tables**\n- Testing multiple similar scenarios\n- Data-driven testing with variations\n- Need to test the same logic with different inputs\n- Want organized, maintainable test cases\n\n## Pattern 1: Testing Standard Error Responses\n\n### Scenario: Testing a 404 Not Found Error\n\nUse this pattern when testing standard HTTP errors.\n\n#### Step-by-Step Guide\n\n**1. Import the necessary fixtures and helpers:**\n\n```python\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import not_found_fixture\nfrom tests.test_helpers import validate_http_status, validate_json_content_type\nfrom tests.test_error_response_validation import validate_standard_error_response\n```\n\n**2. Create the fixture with your test data:**\n\n```python\ndef test_user_not_found():\n    \"\"\"Test 404 response when user doesn't exist.\"\"\"\n    # Create a 404 fixture for the specific endpoint\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n```\n\n**3. Validate the response:**\n\n```python\n    # Validate status code\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n    \n    # Validate content type\n    validate_json_content_type(fixture.to_tuple())\n    \n    # Validate error structure\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n    \n    # Validate specific fields\n    assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n    assert 'not found' in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n```\n\n**4. Run the test:**\n\n```bash\npytest tests/test_my_errors.py::test_user_not_found -v\n```\n\n### Complete Example\n\n```python\ndef test_user_not_found():\n    \"\"\"Test 404 Not Found response for non-existent user.\"\"\"\n    # Create fixture\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n    \n    # Convert to tuple for validation\n    response = fixture.to_tuple()\n    \n    # Validate status code\n    validate_http_status(response, 404)\n    \n    # Validate content type\n    validate_json_content_type(response)\n    \n    # Validate error structure\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n    \n    # Validate specific fields\n    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'not_found'\n    assert fixture.body['code'] == 404\n    assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n    assert 'user' in fixture.body['message'].lower() or '999' in fixture.body['message']\n```\n\n## Pattern 2: Testing Custom Error Responses\n\n### Scenario: Testing a Custom Validation Error\n\nUse this pattern when you need to test errors specific to your API.\n\n#### Step-by-Step Guide\n\n**1. Import the create_error_response builder:**\n\n```python\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import create_error_response\nfrom tests.test_helpers import validate_http_status\n```\n\n**2. Define your custom error structure:**\n\n```python\ndef test_email_validation_error():\n    \"\"\"Test custom email validation error.\"\"\"\n    # Create a custom error response\n    fixture = create_error_response(\n        status=400,\n        error_type=\"validation_error\",\n        message=\"Email address is invalid\",\n        code=400,\n        details={\n            'field': 'email',\n            'value': 'invalid-email',\n            'error': 'Invalid email format'\n        }\n    )\n```\n\n**3. Validate your custom error:**\n\n```python\n    # Validate status code\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 400)\n    \n    # Validate custom fields\n    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'validation_error'\n    assert fixture.body['details']['field'] == 'email'\n    assert 'invalid' in fixture.body['details']['value']\n```\n\n### Complete Example\n\n```python\ndef test_email_validation_error():\n    \"\"\"Test validation error for invalid email format.\"\"\"\n    # Create custom validation error\n    fixture = create_error_response(\n        status=400,\n        error_type=\"validation_error\",\n        message=\"Email address is invalid\",\n        code=400,\n        details={\n            'field': 'email',\n            'provided_value': 'not-an-email',\n            'constraint': 'must be valid email format',\n            'example': 'user@example.com'\n        }\n    )\n    \n    # Basic validation\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 400)\n    \n    # Custom field validation\n    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'validation_error'\n    assert 'email' in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n    assert fixture.body['details']['field'] == 'email'\n    assert '@' not in fixture.body['details']['provided_value']\n    assert 'example.com' in fixture.body['details']['example']\n```\n\n## Pattern 3: Testing Multiple Similar Scenarios\n\n### Scenario: Testing 404 Errors Across Multiple Endpoints\n\nUse this pattern when you have multiple similar test cases.\n\n#### Step-by-Step Guide\n\n**1. Import test table components:**\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_tables import TestCase, create_not_found_test_table, run_test_table\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import not_found_fixture\n```\n\n**2. Define your test cases:**\n\n```python\ndef test_multiple_404_scenarios():\n    \"\"\"Test 404 responses across different endpoints.\"\"\"\n    # Define test cases\n    test_cases = [\n        TestCase(\n            name=\"User not found\",\n            path=\"/api/users/999\",\n            expected_code=\"not_found\"\n        ),\n        TestCase(\n            name=\"Item not found\",\n            path=\"/api/items/abc\",\n            expected_code=\"not_found\"\n        ),\n        TestCase(\n            name=\"Order not found\",\n            path=\"/api/orders/xyz\",\n            expected_code=\"not_found\"\n        ),\n    ]\n```\n\n**3. Create and run the test table:**\n\n```python\n    # Create test table\n    table = create_not_found_test_table(test_cases)\n    \n    # Run all test cases\n    results = []\n    for case in test_cases:\n        # Create fixture for each case\n        fixture = not_found_fixture(path=case.path)\n        \n        # Validate\n        validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n        validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n        \n        results.append(True)\n    \n    # Assert all passed\n    assert all(results)\n```\n\n### Complete Example\n\n```python\ndef test_multiple_not_found_scenarios():\n    \"\"\"Test 404 Not Found for various resource types.\"\"\"\n    # Define test cases\n    scenarios = [\n        (\"/api/users/999\", \"user\"),\n        (\"/api/items/abc\", \"item\"),\n        (\"/api/orders/xyz\", \"order\"),\n        (\"/api/products/invalid\", \"product\"),\n    ]\n    \n    # Test each scenario\n    for path, resource_type in scenarios:\n        # Create fixture\n        fixture = not_found_fixture(path=path)\n        \n        # Validate structure\n        validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n        validate_json_content_type(fixture.to_tuple())\n        validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n        \n        # Validate path is in error details\n        assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == path\n        \n        # Validate resource type is mentioned (optional)\n        # assert resource_type in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n```\n\n## Pattern 4: Testing Live Endpoints\n\n### Scenario: Testing a Real API Endpoint\n\nUse this pattern when testing against a running server.\n\n#### Step-by-Step Guide\n\n**1. Import HTTP request helper:**\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_helpers import make_http_request, validate_http_status\n```\n\n**2. Make the HTTP request:**\n\n```python\ndef test_live_endpoint():\n    \"\"\"Test a live API endpoint.\"\"\"\n    # Make request to test server\n    response = make_http_request(\n        base_url='http://localhost:8080',\n        path='/api/users/999',\n        method='GET'\n    )\n```\n\n**3. Validate the response:**\n\n```python\n    # Check if request succeeded\n    if not response['success']:\n        pytest.skip(f\"Server not available: {response['error']}\")\n    \n    # Validate status code\n    validate_http_status(response['status_code'], 404)\n    \n    # Parse and validate body\n    import json\n    body = json.loads(response['body'])\n    validate_standard_error_response(body)\n```\n\n### Complete Example\n\n```python\n@pytest.mark.integration\ndef test_live_user_endpoint():\n    \"\"\"Test live user endpoint returns proper error for non-existent user.\"\"\"\n    # Make request\n    response = make_http_request(\n        base_url='http://localhost:8080',\n        path='/api/users/999',\n        method='GET',\n        timeout=5\n    )\n    \n    # Skip if server not available\n    if not response['success']:\n        pytest.skip(f\"Server not available: {response['error']}\")\n    \n    # Validate response\n    validate_http_status(response['status_code'], 404)\n    validate_json_content_type((response['status_code'], response['headers'], ''))\n    \n    # Parse and validate body\n    import json\n    body = json.loads(response['body'])\n    validate_standard_error_response(body)\n    \n    # Validate specific fields\n    assert body['error'] == 'not_found'\n    assert body['code'] == 404\n    assert body['details']['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n```\n\n## Pattern 5: Testing HTTP Methods\n\n### Scenario: Testing Method Not Allowed Errors\n\nUse this pattern when testing HTTP method restrictions.\n\n#### Step-by-Step Guide\n\n```python\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import method_not_allowed_fixture\n\ndef test_method_not_allowed():\n    \"\"\"Test 405 Method Not Allowed for DELETE on read-only endpoint.\"\"\"\n    # Create 405 fixture\n    fixture = method_not_allowed_fixture(\n        path=\"/api/stats\",\n        method=\"DELETE\",\n        allowed_methods=[\"GET\", \"HEAD\", \"OPTIONS\"]\n    )\n    \n    # Validate status code\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 405)\n    \n    # Validate Allow header is present\n    assert 'Allow' in fixture.headers\n    assert 'GET' in fixture.headers['Allow']\n    \n    # Validate error body\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'method_not_allowed'\n    assert fixture.body['details']['method'] == 'DELETE'\n```\n\n## Pattern 6: Testing Content-Type Errors\n\n### Scenario: Testing Unsupported Media Type Errors\n\nUse this pattern when testing content-type validation.\n\n```python\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import unsupported_media_type_fixture\n\ndef test_unsupported_media_type():\n    \"\"\"Test 415 Unsupported Media Type for XML request to JSON-only endpoint.\"\"\"\n    # Create 415 fixture\n    fixture = unsupported_media_type_fixture(\n        path=\"/api/users\",\n        content_type=\"application/xml\",\n        supported_types=[\"application/json\"]\n    )\n    \n    # Validate status code\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 415)\n    \n    # Validate error details\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'unsupported_media_type'\n    assert fixture.body['details']['provided_type'] == 'application/xml'\n    assert fixture.body['details']['supported_types'] == ['application/json']\n```\n\n## Pattern 7: Testing Server Errors\n\n### Scenario: Testing Internal Server Error Responses\n\nUse this pattern when testing server error handling.\n\n```python\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import internal_server_error_fixture\n\ndef test_internal_server_error():\n    \"\"\"Test 500 Internal Server Error response.\"\"\"\n    # Create 500 fixture\n    fixture = internal_server_error_fixture(\n        path=\"/api/users\",\n        error_id=\"ERR-DB-001\"\n    )\n    \n    # Validate status code\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 500)\n    \n    # Validate error structure\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n    \n    # Validate server error specifics\n    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'internal_server_error'\n    assert 'error_id' in fixture.body['details']\n    assert fixture.headers.get('X-Error-ID') == 'ERR-DB-001'\n```\n\n## Pattern 8: Testing CORS Headers\n\n### Scenario: Testing CORS Configuration\n\nUse this pattern when testing CORS headers.\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_helpers import validate_cors_headers\n\ndef test_cors_headers():\n    \"\"\"Test CORS headers are properly configured.\"\"\"\n    # Create response with CORS headers\n    response = (\n        200,\n        {\n            'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': 'https://example.com',\n            'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST, OPTIONS',\n            'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials': 'true'\n        },\n        '{}'\n    )\n    \n    # Validate CORS headers\n    validate_cors_headers(\n        response,\n        expected_origin='https://example.com'\n    )\n```\n\n## Pattern 9: Testing Authentication Errors\n\n### Scenario: Testing Authentication/Authorization Errors\n\nUse this pattern when testing auth failures.\n\n```python\nfrom tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import create_error_response\n\ndef test_authentication_error():\n    \"\"\"Test 401 Unauthorized response for missing credentials.\"\"\"\n    # Create 401 fixture\n    fixture = create_error_response(\n        status=401,\n        error_type=\"unauthorized\",\n        message=\"Authentication required\",\n        code=401,\n        details={\n            'auth_type': 'Bearer',\n            'realm': 'api'\n        }\n    )\n    \n    # Validate\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 401)\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n    assert fixture.body['error'] == 'unauthorized'\n```\n\n## Pattern 10: Batch Testing Multiple Fixtures\n\n### Scenario: Testing All Fixtures in a Category\n\nUse this pattern to validate all fixtures of a certain type.\n\n```python\ndef test_all_client_error_fixtures():\n    \"\"\"Test all client error fixtures are valid.\"\"\"\n    # Get all client error fixtures\n    from tests.fixtures.error_scenarios import CLIENT_ERROR_FIXTURES\n    \n    # Test each fixture\n    for name, fixture in CLIENT_ERROR_FIXTURES.items():\n        # Validate status code is in client error range\n        assert 400 <= fixture.status_code < 500, \\\n            f\"{name} has invalid status code {fixture.status_code}\"\n        \n        # Validate error structure\n        validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n        \n        print(f\"✓ {name} validated\")\n```\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### 1. Test Structure\n\nOrganize your tests logically:\n\n```python\nclass TestUserErrors:\n    \"\"\"Tests for user-related error responses.\"\"\"\n    \n    def test_user_not_found(self):\n        \"\"\"Test 404 for non-existent user.\"\"\"\n        pass\n    \n    def test_user_validation_error(self):\n        \"\"\"Test 400 for invalid user data.\"\"\"\n        pass\n    \n    def test_user_unauthorized(self):\n        \"\"\"Test 401 for unauthorized access.\"\"\"\n        pass\n```\n\n### 2. Descriptive Test Names\n\nUse clear, descriptive test names:\n\n```python\n# Good\ndef test_user_not_found_returns_404_with_path_in_details():\n    \"\"\"Test that requesting non-existent user returns 404 with path in error details.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n# Less clear\ndef test_error():\n    pass\n```\n\n### 3. Validation Order\n\nValidate in a logical order:\n\n```python\ndef test_validation_order():\n    \"\"\"Demonstrate proper validation order.\"\"\"\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test\")\n    response = fixture.to_tuple()\n    \n    # 1. Validate status code (fastest check)\n    validate_http_status(response, 404)\n    \n    # 2. Validate content type (medium check)\n    validate_json_content_type(response)\n    \n    # 3. Validate error structure (slower check)\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n    \n    # 4. Validate specific fields (most specific)\n    assert fixture.body['details']['path'] == '/api/test'\n```\n\n### 4. Error Messages\n\nUse assertion messages for clarity:\n\n```python\ndef test_with_clear_messages():\n    \"\"\"Test with clear assertion messages.\"\"\"\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test\")\n    \n    # Clear assertion messages\n    assert fixture.status_code == 404, \\\n        f\"Expected 404, got {fixture.status_code}\"\n    \n    assert 'path' in fixture.body['details'], \\\n        \"Error details should include 'path' field\"\n```\n\n### 5. Test Independence\n\nEnsure tests don't depend on each other:\n\n```python\n# Good - each test is independent\ndef test_scenario_1():\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test1\")\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n\ndef test_scenario_2():\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test2\")\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n\n# Bad - test_scenario_2 depends on test_scenario_1\nshared_fixture = None\n\ndef test_scenario_1():\n    global shared_fixture\n    shared_fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/test\")\n\ndef test_scenario_2():\n    validate_http_status(shared_fixture.to_tuple(), 404)\n```\n\n## Common Testing Scenarios\n\n### Scenario 1: Testing a New Endpoint\n\n```python\ndef test_new_endpoint():\n    \"\"\"Test a new endpoint returns proper error for invalid input.\"\"\"\n    # Create fixture for your endpoint\n    fixture = create_error_response(\n        status=400,\n        error_type=\"invalid_input\",\n        message=\"Invalid input parameter\",\n        code=400,\n        details={'parameter': 'sort', 'value': 'invalid'}\n    )\n    \n    # Validate\n    validate_http_status(fixture.to_tuple(), 400)\n    validate_standard_error_response(fixture.body)\n```\n\n### Scenario 2: Testing Error Messages\n\n```python\ndef test_error_message_content():\n    \"\"\"Test that error messages are user-friendly.\"\"\"\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n    \n    # Check message contains helpful information\n    assert 'users/999' in fixture.body['message'] or \\\n           'user' in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n    \n    # Check message is not too technical\n    assert 'exception' not in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n    assert 'traceback' not in fixture.body['message'].lower()\n```\n\n### Scenario 3: Testing Error Details\n\n```python\ndef test_error_details_completeness():\n    \"\"\"Test that error details include relevant information.\"\"\"\n    fixture = not_found_fixture(path=\"/api/users/999\")\n    \n    # Check required detail fields\n    details = fixture.body['details']\n    assert 'path' in details\n    assert details['path'] == '/api/users/999'\n```\n\n## Running Tests\n\n### Run All Tests\n\n```bash\npytest tests/ -v\n```\n\n### Run Specific Test File\n\n```bash\npytest tests/test_my_errors.py -v\n```\n\n### Run Specific Test\n\n```bash\npytest tests/test_my_errors.py::test_user_not_found -v\n```\n\n### Run with Coverage\n\n```bash\npytest tests/ --cov=tests --cov-report=html\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Common Issues\n\n**1. Import Errors**\n\n```bash\n# Error: ImportError: No module named 'tests.fixtures'\n# Solution: Ensure you're running from the project root\ncd /home/coding/ARMOR\npytest tests/\n```\n\n**2. Fixture Not Found**\n\n```python\n# Error: get_fixture() returns None\n# Solution: Check fixture name spelling and availability\nnames = list_fixtures()\nprint(names)  # Check if your fixture exists\n```\n\n**3. Validation Failures**\n\n```python\n# Error: StatusValidationError\n# Solution: Check your expected vs actual status codes\ntry:\n    validate_http_status(response, 200)\nexcept StatusValidationError as e:\n    print(f\"Expected 200, got {e.actual}\")\n```\n\n## Adding New Fixtures\n\nIf you need a new fixture type:\n\n1. **Add to `fixtures/error_scenarios.py`:**\n\n```python\ndef conflict_fixture(path: str, message: str = None) -> ErrorResponseFixture:\n    \"\"\"Create a 409 Conflict error fixture.\"\"\"\n    return create_error_response(\n        status=409,\n        error_type=\"conflict\",\n        message=message or f\"Resource conflict at {path}\",\n        code=409,\n        details={'path': path, 'conflict_type': 'resource_exists'}\n    )\n```\n\n2. **Export from `__init__.py`:**\n\n```python\nfrom .error_scenarios import conflict_fixture\n```\n\n3. **Add to collection (if applicable):**\n\n```python\nALL_ERROR_FIXTURES['409_conflict'] = conflict_fixture()\n```\n\n## Next Steps\n\n- Review [README.md](README.md) for infrastructure overview\n- Check [example_test.py](example_test.py) for runnable examples\n- See [fixtures/README.md](fixtures/README.md) for fixture documentation\n- Reference [TEST_TABLE_GUIDE.md](TEST_TABLE_GUIDE.md) for test table usage\n\nBead: bf-qxhx20\nCreated: 2026-07-15\n","numLines":771,"startLine":1,"totalLines":771}}}

create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"type-mismatch\")\n867\t\n868\t\n869\t# =============================================================================\n870\t# TEST EXECUTORS\n871\t# =============================================================================\n872\t\n873\tdef validation_test_executor(input_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n874\t    \"\"\"\n875\t    Mock executor for validation error tests.\n876\t\n877\t    In a real implementation, this would:\n878\t    1. Make an actual HTTP request to the endpoint\n879\t    2. Parse the response\n880\t    3. Extract status, error type, message, and fields\n881\t\n882\t    For testing purposes, this simulates expected responses based on input.\n883\t    \"\"\"\n884\t    # Extract input data\n885\t    body = input_data.get(\"body\", {})\n886\t    endpoint = input_data.get(\"endpoint\", \"\")\n887\t    method = input_data.get(\"method\", \"GET\")\n888\t\n889\t    # Check for validation errors in the input\n890\t    # (In a real implementation, this would come from the actual API response)\n891\t\n892\t    # Happy path tests\n893\t    if input_data.get(\"tags\") and \"happy-path\" in input_data.get(\"tags\", []):\n894\t        return {\n895\t            \"status\": 201,\n896\t            \"error\": None,\n897\t            \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n898\t        }\n899\t\n900\t    # Missing required field checks\n901\t    required_field_checks = {\n902\t        \"/api/users\": [\"name\", \"email\"],\n903\t        \"/api/orders\": [\"customer_id\", \"items\"],\n904\t        \"/api/events\": [\"title\", \"date\"]\n905\t    }\n906\t\n907\t    # Check for missing fields\n908\t    if endpoint in required_field_checks:\n909\t        required_fields = required_field_checks[endpoint]\n910\t        missing_fields = [f for f in required_fields if f not in body or body[f] is None]\n911\t\n912\t        if missing_fields:\n913\t            return {\n914\t                \"status\": 422,\n915\t                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n916\t                \"message\": f\"Missing required field(s): {', '.join(missing_fields)}\",\n917\t                \"fields\": {\n918\t                    \"missing_fields\": missing_fields,\n919\t                    \"count\": len(missing_fields)\n920\t                }\n921\t            }\n922\t\n923\t    # Email format check\n924\t    if \"email\" in body:\n925\t        email = body[\"email\"]\n926\t        if \"@\" not in email or \".\" not in email.split(\"@\")[-1]:\n927\t            return {\n928\t                \"status\": 422,\n929\t                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n930\t                \"message\": \"Invalid email format\",\n931\t                \"fields\": {\n932\t                    \"field\": \"email\",\n933\t                    \"provided_value\": email,\n934\t                    \"constraint\": \"must contain @ and valid domain\"\n935\t                }\n936\t            }\n937\t\n938\t    # Age range check\n939\t    if \"age\" in body:\n940\t        age = body[\"age\"]\n941\t        if isinstance(age, str):\n942\t            return {\n943\t                \"status\": 422,\n944\t                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n945\t                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n946\t                \"fields\": {\n947\t                    \"field\": \"age\",\n948\t                    \"expected_type\": \"number\",\n949\t                    \"provided_type\": \"string\",\n950\t                    \"provided_value\": age\n951\t                }\n952\t            }\n953\t        if age < 0 or age > 120:\n954\t            return {\n955\t                \"status\": 422,\n956\t                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n957\t                \"message\": \"age must be between 0 and 120\",\n958\t                \"fields\": {\n959\t                    \"field\": \"age\",\n960\t                    \"provided_value\": age,\n961\t                    \"min_value\": 0,\n962\t                    \"max_value\": 120\n963\t                }\n964\t            }\n965\t\n966\t    # Name length check\n967\t    if \"name\" in body:\n968\t        name = body[\"name\"]\n969\t        if isinstance(name, int):\n970\t            return {\n971\t                \"status\": 422,\n972\t                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n973\t                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n974\t                \"fields\": {\n975\t                    \"field\": \"name\",\n976\t                    \"expected_type\": \"string\",\n977\t                    \"provided_type\": \"number\",\n978\t                    \"provided_value\": name\n979\t                }\n980\t            }\n981\t        if len(name) > 100:\n982\t            return {\n983\t                \"status\": 422,\n984\t                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n985\t                \"message\": \"name too long\",\n986\t                \"fields\": {\n987\t                    \"field\": \"name\",\n988\t                    \"provided_length\": len(name),\n989\t                    \"max_length\": 100\n990\t                }\n991\t            }\n992\t\n993\t    # Date format check\n994\t    if \"date\" in body:\n995\t        date = body[\"date\"]\n996\t        try:\n997\t            from datetime import datetime\n998\t            datetime.fromisoformat(date.replace('Z', '+00:00'))\n999\t        except (ValueError, AttributeError):\n1000\t            return {\n1001\t                \"status\": 422,\n1002\t                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n1003\t                \"message\": \"Invalid date format\",\n1004\t                \"fields\": {\n1005\t                    \"field\": \"date\",\n1006\t                    \"provided_value\": date,\n1007\t                    \"expected_format\": \"ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD)\"\n1008\t                }\n1009\t            }\n1010\t\n1011\t    # Array size check\n1012\t    if \"items\" in body:\n1013\t        items = body[\"items\"]\n1014\t        if isinstance(items, dict):\n1015\t            return {\n1016\t                \"status\": 422,\n1017\t                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n1018\t                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n1019\t                \"fields\": {\n1020\t                    \"field\": \"items\",\n1021\t                    \"expected_type\": \"array\",\n1022\t                    \"provided_type\": \"object\"\n1023\t                }\n1024\t            }\n1025\t        if len(items) > 50:\n1026\t            return {\n1027\t                \"status\": 422,\n1028\t                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n1029\t                \"message\": \"too many items\",\n1030\t                \"fields\": {\n1031\t                    \"field\": \"items\",\n1032\t                    \"provided_size\": len(items),\n1033\t                    \"max_size\": 50\n1034\t                }\n1035\t            }\n1036\t        if len(items) == 0:\n1037\t            return {\n1038\t                \"status\": 422,\n1039\t                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n1040\t                \"message\": \"at least one item required\",\n1041\t                \"fields\": {\n1042\t                    \"field\": \"items\",\n1043\t                    \"provided_size\": 0,\n1044\t                    \"min_size\": 1\n1045\t                }\n1046\t            }\n1047\t\n1048\t    # Default: valid request\n1049\t    return {\n1050\t        \"status\": 201,\n1051\t        \"error\": None,\n1052\t        \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n1053\t    }\n1054\t\n1055\t\n1056\t# =============================================================================\n1057\t# PYTEST TEST CLASSES\n1058\t# create_missing_required_fields_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for missing required field scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"missing-field\")\n\n\ndef create_invalid_format_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for invalid format =============================================================================\n# TEST EXECUTORS\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef validation_test_executor(input_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Mock executor for validation error tests.\n\n    In a real implementation, this would:\n    1. Make an actual HTTP request to the endpoint\n    2. Parse the response\n    3. Extract status, error type, message, and fields\n\n    For testing purposes, this simulates expected responses based on input.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Extract input data\n    body = input_data.get(\"body\", {})\n    endpoint = input_data.get(\"endpoint\", \"\")\n    method = input_data.get(\"method\", \"GET\")\n\n    # Check for validation errors in the input\n    # (In a real implementation, this would come from the actual API response)\n\n    # Happy path tests\n    if input_data.get(\"tags\") and \"happy-path\" in input_data.get(\"tags\", []):\n        return {\n            \"status\": 201,\n            \"error\": None,\n            \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n        }\n\n    # Missing required field checks\n    required_field_checks = {\n        \"/api/users\": [\"name\", \"email\"],\n        \"/api/orders\": [\"customer_id\", \"items\"],\n        \"/api/events\": [\"title\", \"date\"]\n    }\n\n    # Check for missing fields\n    if endpoint in required_field_checks:\n        required_fields = required_field_checks[endpoint]\n        missing_fields = [f for f in required_fields if f not in body or body[f] is None]\n\n        if missing_fields:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": f\"Missing required field(s): {', '.join(missing_fields)}\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"missing_fields\": missing_fields,\n                    \"count\": len(missing_fields)\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Email format check\n    if \"email\" in body:\n        email = body[\"email\"]\n        if \"@\" not in email or \".\" not in email.split(\"@\")[-1]:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid email format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"email\",\n                    \"provided_value\": email,\n                    \"constraint\": \"must contain @ and valid domain\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Age range check\n    if \"age\" in body:\n        age = body[\"age\"]\n        if isinstance(age, str):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age\n                }\n            }\n        if age < 0 or age > 120:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"age must be between 0 and 120\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age,\n                    \"min_value\": 0,\n                    \"max_value\": 120\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Name length check\n    if \"name\" in body:\n        name = body[\"name\"]\n        if isinstance(name, int):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_value\": name\n                }\n            }\n        if len(name) > 100:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"name too long\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"provided_length\": len(name),\n                    \"max_length\": 100\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Date format check\n    if \"date\" in body:\n        date = body[\"date\"]\n        try:\n            from datetime import datetime\n            datetime.fromisoformat(date.replace('Z', '+00:00'))\n        except (ValueError, AttributeError):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid date format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"date\",\n                    \"provided_value\": date,\n                    \"expected_format\": \"ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD)\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Array size check\n    if \"items\" in body:\n        items = body[\"items\"]\n        if isinstance(items, dict):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"array\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"object\"\n                }\n            }\n        if len(items) > 50:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"too many items\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"provided_size\": len(items),\n                    \"max_size\": 50\n                }\n            }\n        if len(items) == 0:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"at least one item required\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"provided_size\": 0,\n                    \"min_size\": 1\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Default: valid request\n    return {\n        \"status\": 201,\n        \"error\": None,\n        \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n    }\n\n\n#    print(f\"Skipped: {results.skipped_count}\")\n        print(f\"Errors: {results.error_count}\")\n        print(f\"Pass Rate: {results.pass_rate:.1f}%\")\n        print(f\"Execution Time: {results.execution_time_ms:.2f}ms\")\n\n        # Show failed tests if any\n        if results.failed_count > 0:\n            print(f\"\\nFailed Tests:\")\n            for result in results.results:\n                if result.failed:\n                    print(f\"  ✗ {result.test_case.id}: {result.test_case.description}\")\n                    print(f\"    Error: {result.error_message}\")\n\n        assert results.all_passed, f\"Some tests failed: {results.failed_count} failures\"\n\n    def test_validation_test_table_structure(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify the validation test table has proper structure.\"\"\"\n        table = create_comprehensive_validation_test_table()\n\n        # Check table properties\n        assert table.name == \"comprehensive_validation_errors\"\n        assert table.description is not None\n        assert len(table.test_cases) > 0\n\n        # Check that we have tests for all required categories\n        tags = set()\n        for test_case in table.test_cases:\n            tags.update(test_case.tags)\n\n        required_categories = [\"missing-field\", \"invalid-format\", \"out-of-range\", \"type-mismatch\"]\n        for category in required_categories:\n            assert category in tags, f\"Missing test category: {category}\"\n\n        # Check that all test cases have proper structure\n        for test_case in table.test_cases:\n            assert test_case.id, f\"Test case missing ID: {test_case.description}\"\n            assert test_case.description, \"Test case missing description\"\n            assert test_case.input_data, f\"Test case missing input_data: {test_case.id}\"\n            assert test_case.expected_status is not None, f\"Test case missing expected_status: {test_case.id}\"\n            assert len(test_case.tags) > 0, f\"Test case has no tags: {test_case.id}\"\n\n        print(f\"✓ Validation test table structure validated\")\n        print(f\"  Total test cases: {len(table.test_cases)}\")\n        print(f\"  Categories covered: {required_categories}\")\n\n    def test_test_cases_are_runnable(self):\n        \"\"\"Verify that all test cases can be executed successfully.\"\"\"\n        table = create_comprehensive_validation_test_table()\n\n        # Try to run each test case\n        for test_case in table.test_cases:\n            result = FUNCTIONS\n351\t# =============================================================================\n352\t\n353\tdef run_test_case(\n354\t    test_case: TestCase,\n355\t    executor: Callable[[Dict[str,            if actual_status != test_case.expected_status:\n396\t                return TestExecutionResult(\n397\t                    test_case=test_case,\n398\t                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n399\t                    actual_status=actual_status,\n400\t                    actual_error=actual_error,\n401\t                    actual_message=actual_message,\n402\t                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n403\t                    error_message=f\"Expected status {test_case.expected_status}, got {actual_status}\"\n404\t                )\n405\t\n406\t        # Validate expected error\n407\t        if test_case.expected_error is not None:\n408\t            if actual_error != test_case.expected_error:\n409\t                return TestExecutionResult(\n410\t                    test_case=test_case,\n411\t                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n412\t                    actual_status=actual_status,\n413\t                    actual_error=actual_error,\n414\t                    actual_message=actual_message,\n415\t                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n416\t                    error_message=f\"Expected error '{test_case.expected_error}', got '{actual_error}'\"\n417\t                )\n418\t\n419\t        # Validate expected message\n420\t        if test_case.expected_message is not None:\n421\t            if actual_message is None or test_case.expected_message not in actual_message:\n422\t                return TestExecutionResult(\n423\t                    test_case=test_case,\n424\t                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n425\t                    actual_status=actual_status,\n426\t                    actual_error=actual_error,\n427\t                    actual_message=actual_message,\n428\t                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n429\t                    error_message=f\"Expected message containing '{test_case.expected_message}', got '{actual_message}'\"\n430\t                )\n431\t\n432\t        # Validate expected fields\n433\t        if test_case.expected_fields is not None:\n434\t            if actual_fields is None:\n435\t                return TestExecutionResult(\n436\t                    test_case=test_case,\n437\t                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n438\t                    actual_status=actual_status,\n439\t                    actual_error=actual_error,\n440\t                    actual_message=actual_message,\n441\t                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n442\t                    error_message=f\"Expected fields {test_case.expected_fields}, but no fields returned\"\n443\t                )\n444\t            for key, expected_value in test_case.expected_fields.items():\n445\t                if key not in actual_fields:\n446\t                    return TestExecutionResult(\n447\t                        test_case=test_case,\n448\t                        result=TestResult.FAILED,\n449\t                        actual_status=actual_status,\n450\t                        actual_error=actual_error,\n451\t                        actual_message=actual_message,\n452\t                        actual_fields=actual_fields,\n453\t                        error_message=f\"Missing expected field: {key}\"\n454\t                    )\n455\t                if actual_fields[key] != expected_value:\n456\t                    return TestExecutionResult(\n457\t                        test_case=test_case,\n458\t                        result=TestResult.FAILED,\n459\t                        actual_status=actual_status,\n460\t                        actual_error=actual_error,\n461\t                        actual_message=actual_message,\n462\t                        actual_fields=actual_fields,\n463\t                        error_message=f\"Field '{key}' expected {expected_value}, got {actual_fields[key]}\"\n464\t                    )\n465\t\n466\t        # Run custom validator if provided\n467\t        if test_case.custom_validator:\n468\t            passed, error_msg = test_case.custom_validator(actual)\n469\t            if not passed:\n470\t                return TestExecutionResult(\n471\t                    test_case=test_case,\n472\t                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n473\t                    actual_status=actual_status,\n474\t                    actual_error=actual_error,\n475\t                    actual_message=actual_message,\n476\t                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n477\t                    error_message=error_msg or \"Custom validation failed\"\n478\t                )\n479\t\n480\t        # Test passed\n481\t        execution_time = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000\n482\t        return TestExecutionResult(\n483\t            test_case=test_case,\n484\t            result=TestResult.PASSED,\n485\t            actual_status=actual_status,\n486\t            actual_error=actual_error,\n487\t            actual_message=actual_message,\n488\t            actual_fields=actual_fields,\n489\t            execution_time_ms=execution_time\n490\t        )\n491\t\n492\t    except Exception as e:\n493\t        # Test error\n494\t        execution_time = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000\n495\t        return TestExecutionResult(\n496\t            test_case=test_case,\n497\t            result=TestResult.ERROR,\n498\t            error_message=f\"Test execution error: {str(e)}\",\n499\t            execution_time_ms=execution_time\n500\t        )\n501\t\n502\t    finally:\n503\t        # Run teardown callback if provided\n504\t        if test_case.teardown_callback:\n505\t            try:\n506\t                test_case.teardown_callback(test_case.input_data)\n507\t            except Exception as e:\n508\t                # Don't fail the test on teardown error, just log it\n509\t                pass\n510\t\n511\t\n512\tdef run_test_table(\n513\t    table: ErrorTestTable,\n514\t    executor: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Dict[str, Any]],\n515\t    continue_on_failure: bool = True\n516\t) -> TestTableResult:\n517\t    \"\"\"\n518\t    Run all test cases in a test table.\n519\t\n520\t    Args:\n521\t        table: The test table to run\n522\t        executor: Function that executes test cases and returns results\n523\t        continue_on_failure: If True, continues running tests after failures\n524\t\n525\t    Returns:\n526\t        TestTableResult: Aggregated results from all test cases\n527\t    \"\"\"\n528\t    import time\n529\t\n530\t    start_time = time.time()\n531\t    results = []\n532\t\n533\t    # Run table setup callback\n534\t    if table.setup_callback:\n535\t        try:\n536\t            table.setup_callback()\n537\t        except Exception as e:\n538\t            # If table setup fails, return error result\n539\t            return TestTableResult(\n540\t                table_name=table.name,\n541\t                total_count=len(table.test_cases),\n542\t                passed_count=0,\n543\t                failed_count=0,\n544\t                skipped_count=len(table.test_cases),\n545\t                error_count=len(table.test_cases),\n546\t                execution_time_ms=(time.time() - start_time) * 1000\n547\t            )\n548\t\n549\t    # Run each test case\n550\t    for test_case in table.test_cases:\n551\t        result = run_test_case(test_case, executor)\n552\t        results.append(result)\n553\t\n554\t        # Stop on first failure if configured\n555\t        if not continue_on_failure and result.result in (TestResult.FAILED, TestResult.ERROR):\n556\t            # Mark remaining tests as skipped\n557\t            for remaining_case in table.test_cases[len(results):]:\n558\t                results.append(TestExecutionResult(\n559\t                    test_case=remaining_case,\n560\t                    result=TestResult.SKIPPED\n561\t                ))\n562\t            break\n563\t\n564\t    # Run table teardown callback\n565\t    if table.teardown_callback:\n566\t        try:\n567\t            table.teardown_callback()\n568\t        except Exception as e:\n569\t            pass  # Don't fail the table run on teardown error\n570\t\n571\t    # Calculate summary statistics\n572\t    passed_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.result == TestResult.PASSED)\n573\t    failed_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.result == TestResult.FAILED)\n574\t    skipped_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.result == TestResult.SKIPPED)\n575\t    error_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.result == TestResult.ERROR)\n576\t\n577\t    return TestTableResult(\n578\t        table_name=table.name,\n579\t        total_count=len(results),\n580\t        passed_count=passed_count,\n581\t        failed_count=failed_count,\n582\t        skipped_count=skipped_count,\n583\t        error_count=error_count,\n584\t        results=results,\n585\t        execution_time_ms=(time.time() - start_time) * 1000\n586\t    )\n587\t\n588\t\n589\t# =============================================================================\n590\t# CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR CREATING TEST TABLES\n591\t# =============================================================================\n592\t\n593\tdef create_simple_test_table(\n594\t    name: str,\n595\t    description: str,\n596\t    test_data: List[Tuple[str, str, Dict[str, Any], int, str, str]]\n597\t) -> ErrorTestTable:\n598\t    \"\"\"\n599\t    Create a test table from a simple list format.\n600\t\n601\t    Args:\n602\t        name: Table name\n603\t        description: Table description\n604\t        test_data: List of tuples in format:\n605\t                   (id, description, input_data, expected_status, expected_error, expected_message)\n606\t\n607\t    Returns:\n608\t        ErrorTestTable: Configured test table\n609\t    \"\"\"\n610\t    test_cases = []\n611\t    for i, (test_id, desc, inputs, status, error, message) in enumerate(test_data, 1):\n612\t        test_cases.append(TestCase(\n613\t            id=test_id,\n614\t            description=desc,\n615\t            input_data=inputs,\n616\t            expected_status=status,\n617\t            expected_error=error,\n618\t            expected_message=message\n619\t        ))\n620\t\n621\t    return ErrorTestTable(\n622\t        name=name,\n623\t        description=description,\n624\t        test_cases=test_cases\n625\t    )\n626\t\n627\t\n628\tdef create_auth_test_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n629\t    \"\"\"\n630\t    Create a comprehensive test name=\"server_errors\",\n957\t        description=\"Test cases for server error (5xx) responses\",\n958\t        tags=[\"5xx\", \"server_error\", \"critical\"],\n959\t        test_cases=[\n960\t            TestCase(\n961\t                id=\"SE-001\",\n962\t                description=\"Internal server error\",\n963\t                input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/crash\", \"simulate\": \"internal_error\"},\n964\t                expected_status=500,\n965\t                expected_error=\"internal_server_error\",\n966\t                expected_message=\"Internal server error\",\n967\t                tags=[\"smoke\"]\n968\t            ),\n969\t            TestCase(\n970\t                id=\"SE-002\",\n971\t                description=\"Database connection timeout\",\n972\t python3\n\"\"\"\nExtensible Test Table Structure for ARMOR Tests\n\nThis module provides a reusable, table-driven testing framework for ARMOR test cases.\nTest tables allow you to define test cases in a structured, declarative way that can\nbe easily extended for different error types and scenarios.\n\nStructure:\n- TestCase: Individual test case with inputs, expected outputs, and metadata\n- ErrorTestTable: Collection of test cases for a specific error type or scenario\n- Test execution functions: Run tests from tables with proper validation\n\nBenefits:\n- DRY: Define test patterns once, reuse across multiple test cases\n- Extensible: Easy to add new test cases by adding rows to the table\n- Maintainable: Clear separation between test data and test logic\n- Documented: Test tables serve as living documentation of expected behavior\n\nUsage Example:\n    >>> # Define a test table for authentication errors\n    >>> auth_table = ErrorTestTable(\n    ...     name=\"Authentication Errors\",\n    ...     description=\"Test cases for authentication-related error responses\",\n    ...     test_cases=[\n    ... ]\n    ... )\n    >>>\n    >>> # Run all tests in the table\n    >>> results = run_test_table(auth_table)\n    >>> print(f\"Passed: {results.passed_count}, Failed: {results.failed_count}\")\n\nBead: TestCase:\n    \"\"\"\n    A single test case definition.\n\n    Represents one row in a test table with all the information needed\n    to execute the test and validate the result.\n\n    Attributes:\n        id: Unique identifier for this test case (e.g., \"AUTH-001\", \"VAL-001\")\n        description: Human-readable description of what this test validates\n        input_data: Input is None:\n            raise ValueError(\"Test case must have input_data\")\n\n    @property\n    def is_http_test(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if this is an HTTP status test.\"\"\"\n        return self.expected_status is not None\n\n    @property\n    def is_error_test(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if this tests error responses.\"\"\"\n        return self.expected_error is not None\n\n    def with_tags(self, *tags: str) -> 'TestCase':\n        \"\"\"Return a new test case with additional tags.\"\"\"\n        new_tags = list(set(self.tags + list(tags)))\n        return TestCase(\n            id=self.id,\n            description=self.description,\n            input_data=self.input_data,\n            expected_status=self.expected_status,\n
