Contains the outcome, actual vs expected values, and any error messages.\n    \"\"\"\n    test_case: TestCase\n    result: TestResult\n    actual_status: Optional[int] = None\n    actual_error: Optional[str] = None\n    actual_message: Optional[str] = None\n    actual_fields: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None\n    error_message: Optional[str] = None\n    execution_time_ms: Optional[float] = None\n\n    @property\n    def passed(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if the test passed.\"\"\"\n        return self.result == TestResult.PASSED\n\n    @property\n    def failed(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if the test failed.\"\"\"\n        return self.result == TestResult.FAILED\n\n    @property\n    def skipped(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if the test was skipped.\"\"\"\n        return self.result == TestResult.SKIPPED\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Convert result to dictionary for serialization.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            'test_id': self.test_case.id,\n            'description': self.test_case.description,\n            'result': self.result.value,\n            'expected_status': self.test_case.expected_status,\n            'actual_status': self.actual_status,\n            'expected_error': self.test_case.expected_error,\n            'actual_error': self.actual_error,\n            'expected_message': self.test_case.expected_message,\n            'actual_message': self.actual_message,\n            'error_message': self.error_message,\n            'execution_time_ms': self.execution_time_ms\n        }\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass TestTableResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Aggregated results from running a test table.\n\n    Contains summary statistics and individual test case results.\n    \"\"\"\n    table_name: str\n    total_count: int\n    passed_count: int\n    failed_count: int\n    skipped_count: int\n    error_count: int\n    results: List[TestExecutionResult] = field(default_factory=list)\n    execution_time_ms: Optional[float] = None\n\n    @property\n    def all_passed(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if all tests passed.\"\"\"\n        return self.failed_count == 0 and self.error_count == 0\n\n    @property\n    def pass_rate(self) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Calculate pass rate as a percentage.\"\"\"\n        if self.total_count == 0:\n            return 0.0\n        return (self.passed_count / self.total_count) * 100\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Convert results to dictionary for serialization.\"\"\"\n self.skipped_count,\n            'error': self.error_count,\n            'pass_rate': self.pass_rate,\n            'execution_time_ms': self.execution_time_ms,\n            'results': [r.to_dict() for together and provide metadata\n    about what category of errors they test (authentication, validation, etc.).\n\n    Attributes:\n        name: Unique name for this test table (e.g., \"auth_errors\", \"validation_errors\")\n        description: Human-readable description of what this table tests\n        test_cases: List of test case definitions\n        tags: Optional list of tags for categorization\n        setup_callback: Optional callback to run before the entire table\n        teardown_callback: Optional callback to run after the entire table\n        metadata: Optional dict for additional table metadata\n    \"\"\"\n\n    name: str\n    description: str\n    test_cases: List[TestCase] = field(default_factory=list)\n    tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n    setup_callback: Optional[Callable] = None\n    teardown_callback: Optional[Callable] = None\n    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def __post_init__(self):\n        \"\"\"Validate test table after initialization.\"\"\"\n        if not self.name:\n            raise ValueError(\"Test table must have a name\")\n        if not self.description:\n            raise ValueError(\"Test table must have a description\")\n\n        # Ensure test case IDs are unique within the table\n        ids = [tc.id for tc in self.test_cases]\n        if len(ids) != len(set(ids)):\n            duplicates = [id for id in ids if ids.count(id) > 1]\n            raise ValueError(f\"Duplicate test case IDs found: {set(duplicates)}\")\n\n        # Initialize default test_cases if None\n        if self.test_cases is None:\n            self.test_cases = []\n\n    def add_test_case(self, test_case: TestCase) -> 'ErrorTestTable':\n        \"\"\"Add a test case to this table.\"\"\"\n        # Check for duplicate ID\n        existing_ids = {tc.id for tc in self.test_cases}\n        if test_case.id in existing_ids:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Test case ID '{test_case.id}' already exists in table\")\n\n        self.test_cases.append(test_case)\n        return self\n\n    def get_test_case(self, test_id: str) -> Optional[TestCase]:\n        \"\"\"Get a test case by ID.\"\"\"\n        for tc in self.test_cases:\n            if tc.id == test_id:\n                return tc\n        return None\n\n    def get_test_cases_by_tag(self, tag: str) -> List[TestCase]:\n        \"\"\"Get all test cases with a specific tag.\"\"\"\n        return [tc for tc in self.test_cases if tag in tc.tags]\n\n    def get_enabled_test_cases(self) -> List[TestCase]:\n        \"\"\"Get only enabled test cases.\"\"\"\n        return [tc for tc in self.test_cases if tc.enabled]\n\n    def filter_by_tags(self, *tags: str) -> 'ErrorTestTable':\n        \"\"\"Create a new table with only test cases having all specified tags.\"\"\"\n        filtered_cases = [\n            tc for tc in self.test_cases\n            if all(tag in tc.tags for tag in tags)\n        ]\n        return ErrorTestTable(\n            name=f\"{self.name}_filtered\",\n            description=f\"Filtered view of {self.name}\",\n            test_cases=filtered_cases,\n            tags=self.tags.copy(),\n            metadata=self.metadata.copy()\n        )\n\n    def merge(self, other: 'ErrorTestTable') -> 'ErrorTestTable':\n        \"\"\"Merge another test table into this one.\"\"\"\n        # Check for duplicate IDs\n        self_ids = {tc.id for tc in self.test_cases}\n        other_ids = {tc.id for tc in other.test_cases}\n        overlaps = self_ids & other_ids\n        if overlaps:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Cannot merge tables with overlapping test case IDs: {overlaps}\")\n\n        self.test_cases.extend(other.test_cases)\n        return self\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# TEST EXECUTION FUNCTIONS\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef run_test_case(\n    test_case: TestCase,\n    executor: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Dict[str, Any]]\n) -> TestExecutionResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Execute a single test case.\n\n    Args:\n        test_case: The test case to execute\n        executor: Function that takes input_data and returns actual results\n                 Should return dict with optional keys: status, error, message, fields\n\n    Returns:\n        TestExecutionResult: The execution result with pass/fail status\n    \"\"\"\n    import time\n\n    # Check if test is enabled\n    if not test_case.enabled:\n        return TestExecutionResult(\n            test_case=test_case,\n            result=TestResult.SKIPPED\n        )\n\n    start_time = time.time()\n\n    try:\n        # Run setup callback if provided\n        if test_case.setup_callback:\n            test_case.setup_callback(test_case.input_data)\n\n        # Execute the test\n        actual = executor(test_case.input_data)\n\n        # Extract actual values\n        actual_status = actual.get('status')\n        actual_error = actual.get('error')\n        actual_message = actual.get('message')\n        actual_fields = actual.get('fields')\n\n        # Validate expected status\n        if test_case.expected_status is not None:\n            if actual_status != test_case.expected_status:\n                return TestExecutionResult(\n                    test_case=test_case,\n                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n                    actual_status=actual_status,\n                    actual_error=actual_error,\n                    actual_message=actual_message,\n                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n                    error_message=f\"Expected status {test_case.expected_status}, got {actual_status}\"\n                )\n\n        # Validate expected error\n        if test_case.expected_error is not None:\n            if actual_error != test_case.expected_error:\n                return TestExecutionResult(\n                    test_case=test_case,\n                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n                    actual_status=actual_status,\n                    actual_error=actual_error,\n                    actual_message=actual_message,\n                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n                    error_message=f\"Expected error '{test_case.expected_error}', got '{actual_error}'\"\n                )\n\n        # Validate expected message\n        if test_case.expected_message is not None:\n            if actual_message is None or test_case.expected_message not in actual_message:\n                return TestExecutionResult(\n                    test_case=test_case,\n                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n                    actual_status=actual_status,\n                    actual_error=actual_error,\n                    actual_message=actual_message,\n                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n                    error_message=f\"Expected message containing '{test_case.expected_message}', got '{actual_message}'\"\n                )\n\n        # Validate expected fields\n        if test_case.expected_fields is not None:\n            if actual_fields is None:\n                return TestExecutionResult(\n                    test_case=test_case,\n                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n                    actual_status=actual_status,\n                    actual_error=actual_error,\n                    actual_message=actual_message,\n                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n                    error_message=f\"Expected fields {test_case.expected_fields}, but no fields returned\"\n                )\n            for key, expected_value in test_case.expected_fields.items():\n                if key not in actual_fields:\n                    return TestExecutionResult(\n                        test_case=test_case,\n                        result=TestResult.FAILED,\n                        actual_status=actual_status,\n                        actual_error=actual_error,\n                        actual_message=actual_message,\n                        actual_fields=actual_fields,\n                        error_message=f\"Missing expected field: {key}\"\n                    )\n                if actual_fields[key] != expected_value:\n                    return TestExecutionResult(\n                        test_case=test_case,\n                        result=TestResult.FAILED,\n                        actual_status=actual_status,\n                        actual_error=actual_error,\n                        actual_message=actual_message,\n                        actual_fields=actual_fields,\n                        error_message=f\"Field '{key}' expected {expected_value}, got {actual_fields[key]}\"\n                    )\n\n        # Run custom validator if provided\n        if test_case.custom_validator:\n            passed, error_msg = test_case.custom_validator(actual)\n            if not passed:\n                return TestExecutionResult(\n                    test_case=test_case,\n                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n                    actual_status=actual_status,\n                    actual_error=actual_error,\n                    actual_message=actual_message,\n                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n                    error_message=error_msg or \"Custom validation failed\"\n                )\n\n        # Test passed\n        execution_time = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000\n        return TestExecutionResult(\n            test_case=test_case,\n            result=TestResult.PASSED,\n            actual_status=actual_status,\n            actual_error=actual_error,\n            actual_message=actual_message,\n            actual_fields=actual_fields,\n            execution_time_ms=execution_time\n        )\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        # Test error\n        execution_time = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000\n        return TestExecutionResult(\n            test_case=test_case,\n            result=TestResult.ERROR,\n            error_message=f\"Test execution error: {str(e)}\",\n            execution_time_ms=execution_time\n        )\n\n    finally:\n        # Run teardown callback if provided\n        if test_case.teardown_callback:\n            try:\n                test_case.teardown_callback(test_case.input_data)\n            except Exception as e:\n                # Don't fail the test on teardown error, just log it\n                pass\n\n\ndef run_test_table(\n    table: ErrorTestTable,\n    executor: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Dict[str, Any]],\n    continue_on_failure: bool = True\n) -> TestTableResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run all test cases in a test table.\n\n    Args:\n        table: The test table to run\n        executor: Function that executes test cases and returns results\n        continue_on_failure: If True, continues running tests after failures\n\n    Returns:\n        TestTableResult: Aggregated results from all test cases\n    \"\"\"\n    import time\n\n    start_time = time.time()\n    results = []\n\n    # Run table setup callback\n    if table.setup_callback:\n        try:\n            table.setup_callback()\n        except Exception as e:\n            # If table setup fails, return error result\n            return TestTableResult(\n                table_name=table.name,\n                total_count=len(table.test_cases),\n                passed_count=0,\n                failed_count=0,\n                skipped_count=len(table.test_cases),\n                error_count=len(table.test_cases),\n                execution_time_ms=(time.time() - start_time) * 1000\n            )\n\n    # Run each test case\n    for test_case in table.test_cases:\n        result = run_test_case(test_case, executor)\n        results.append(result)\n\n        # Stop on first failure if configured\n        if not continue_on_failure and result.result in (TestResult.FAILED, TestResult.ERROR):\n            # Mark remaining tests as skipped\n            for remaining_case in table.test_cases[len(results):]:\n                results.append(TestExecutionResult(\n                    test_case=remaining_case,\n                    result=TestResult.SKIPPED\n                ))\n            break\n\n    # Run table teardown callback\n    if table.teardown_callback:\n        try:\n            table.teardown_callback()\n        except Exception as e:\n            pass  # Don't fail the table run on teardown error\n\n    # Calculate summary statistics\n    passed_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.result == TestResult.PASSED)\n    failed_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.result == TestResult.FAILED)\n    skipped_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.result == TestResult.SKIPPED)\n    error_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.result == TestResult.ERROR)\n\n    return TestTableResult(\n        table_name=table.name,\n        total_count=len(results),\n        passed_count=passed_count,\n        failed_count=failed_count,\n        skipped_count=skipped_count,\n        error_count=error_count,\n        results=results,\n        execution_time_ms=(time.time() - start_time) * 1000\n    )\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR CREATING TEST TABLES\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef create_simple_test_table(\n    name: str,\n    description: str,\n    test_data: List[Tuple[str, str, Dict[str, Any], int, str, str]]\n) -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"\n    Create a test table from a simple list format.\n\n    Args:\n        name: Table name\n        description: Table description\n        test_data: List of tuples in format:\n                   (id, description, input_data, expected_status, expected_error, expected_message)\n\n    Returns:\n        ErrorTestTable: Configured test table\n    \"\"\"\n    test_cases = []\n    for i, (test_id, desc, inputs, status, error, message) in enumerate(test_data, 1):\n        test_cases.append(TestCase(\n            id=test_id,\n            description=desc,\n            input_data=inputs,\n            expected_status=status,\n            expected_error=error,\n            expected_message=message\n        ))\n\n    return ErrorTestTable(\n        name=name,\n        description=description,\n        test_cases=test_cases\n    )\n\n\ndef create_auth_test_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"\n    Create a comprehensive test table for authentication error scenarios.\n\n    Returns:\n        ErrorTestTable: Pre-configured authentication error test table\n\n    This table covers:\n    - Missing credentials (no API key, no token)\n    - Invalid credentials (wrong format, revoked)\n    - Expired credentials (token expiration)\n    - Wrong password (basic auth)\n    - Insufficient permissions (RBAC)\n    \"\"\"\n    return ErrorTestTable(\n        name=\"authentication_errors\",\n        description=\"Test cases for authentication and authorization error responses\",\n        tags=[\"auth\", \"security\", \"critical\"],\n        test_cases=[\n            # Missing credentials test cases\n            TestCase(\n                id=\"AUTH-001\",\n                description=\"Missing API key in request headers\",\n                input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"headers\": {}},\n                expected_status=401,\n                expected_error=\"unauthorized\",\n                expected_message=\"API key required\",\n                tags=[\"smoke\", \"missing-credentials\"]\n            ),\n            TestCase(\n                id=\"AUTH-002\",\n                description=\"Missing Bearer token in Authorization header\",\n                input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"headers\": {\"Authorization\": \"\"}},\n                expected_status=401,\n                expected_error=\"unauthorized\",\n                expected_message=\"Authentication required\",\n                tags=[\"missing-credentials\", \"bearer\"]\n            ),\n            TestCase(\n                id=\"AUTH-003\",\n                description=\"Missing Basic auth credentials\",\n                input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/auth/login\", \"headers\": {}},\n                expected_status=401,\n                expected_error=\"unauthorized\",\n                expected_message=\"Authentication required\",\n =============================================================================\n# TEST EXECUTORS\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef validation_test_executor(input_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Mock executor for validation error tests.\n\n    In a real implementation, this would:\n    1. Make an actual HTTP request to the endpoint\n    2. Parse the response\n    3. Extract status, error type, message, and fields\n\n    For testing purposes, this simulates expected responses based on input.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Extract input data\n    body = input_data.get(\"body\", {})\n    endpoint = input_data.get(\"endpoint\", \"\")\n    method = input_data.get(\"method\", \"GET\")\n\n    # Check for validation errors in the input\n    # (In a real implementation, this would come from the actual API response)\n\n    # Happy path tests\n    if input_data.get(\"tags\") and \"happy-path\" in input_data.get(\"tags\", []):\n        return {\n            \"status\": 201,\n            \"error\": None,\n            \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n        }\n\n    # Missing required field checks\n    required_field_checks = {\n        \"/api/users\": [\"name\", \"email\"],\n        \"/api/orders\": [\"customer_id\", \"items\"],\n        \"/api/events\": [\"title\", \"date\"]\n    }\n\n    # Check for missing fields\n    if endpoint in required_field_checks:\n        required_fields = required_field_checks[endpoint]\n        missing_fields = [f for f in required_fields if f not in body or body[f] is None]\n\n        if missing_fields:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": f\"Missing required field(s): {', '.join(missing_fields)}\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"missing_fields\": missing_fields,\n                    \"count\": len(missing_fields)\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Email format check\n    if \"email\" in body:\n        email = body[\"email\"]\n        if \"@\" not in email or \".\" not in email.split(\"@\")[-1]:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid email format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"email\",\n                    \"provided_value\": email,\n                    \"constraint\": \"must contain @ and valid domain\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Age range check\n    if \"age\" in body:\n        age = body[\"age\"]\n        if isinstance(age, str):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age\n                }\n            }\n        if age < 0 or age > 120:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"age must be between 0 and 120\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age,\n                    \"min_value\": 0,\n                    \"max_value\": 120\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Name length check\n    if \"name\" in body:\n        name = body[\"name\"]\n        if isinstance(name, int):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_value\": name\n                }\n            }\n        if len(name) > 100:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"name too long\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"provided_length\": len(name),\n                    \"max_length\": 100\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Date format check\n    if \"date\" in body:\n        date = body[\"date\"]\n        try:\n            from datetime import datetime\n            datetime.fromisoformat(date.replace('Z', '+00:00'))\n        except (ValueError, AttributeError):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid date format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"date\",\n                    \"provided_value\": date,\n                    \"expected_format\": \"ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD)\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Array size check\n    if \"items\" in body:\n        items = body[\"items\"]\n        if isinstance(items, dict):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"array\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"object\"\n                }\n            }\n        if len(items) > 50:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"too many items\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"provided_size\": len(items),\n                    \"max_size\": 50\n                }\n }\n\n    # Default: valid request\n    return {\n        \"status\": 201,\n        \"error\": None,\n        \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n    }\n\n\n# create_missing_required_fields_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for missing required field scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"missing-field\")\n\n\ndef create_invalid_format_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for invalid format scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"invalid-format\")\n\n\ndef create_out_of_range_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for out-of-range value scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"out-of-range\")\n\n\ndef create_type_mismatch_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for type mismatch scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"type-mismatch\")\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# TEST EXECUTORS\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef validation_test_executor(input_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Mock executor for validation error tests.\n\n    In a real implementation, this would:\n    1. Make an actual HTTP request to the endpoint\n    2. Parse the response\n    3. Extract status, error type, message, and fields\n\n    For testing purposes, this simulates expected responses based on input.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Extract input data\n    body = input_data.get(\"body\", {})\n    endpoint = input_data.get(\"endpoint\", \"\")\n    method = input_data.get(\"method\", \"GET\")\n\n    # Check for validation errors in the input\n    # (In a real implementation, this would come from the actual API response)\n\n    # Happy path tests\n    if input_data.get(\"tags\") and \"happy-path\" in input_data.get(\"tags\", []):\n        return {\n            \"status\": 201,\n            \"error\": None,\n            \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n        }\n\n    # Missing required field checks\n    required_field_checks = {\n        \"/api/users\": [\"name\", \"email\"],\n        \"/api/orders\": [\"customer_id\", \"items\"],\n        \"/api/events\": [\"title\", \"date\"]\n    }\n\n    # Check for missing fields\n    if endpoint in required_field_checks:\n        required_fields = required_field_checks[endpoint]\n        missing_fields = [f for f in required_fields if f not in body or body[f] is None]\n\n        if missing_fields:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": f\"Missing required field(s): {', '.join(missing_fields)}\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"missing_fields\": missing_fields,\n                    \"count\": len(missing_fields)\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Email format check\n    if \"email\" in body:\n        email = body[\"email\"]\n        if \"@\" not in email or \".\" not in email.split(\"@\")[-1]:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid email format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"email\",\n                    \"provided_value\": email,\n                    \"constraint\": \"must contain @ and valid domain\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Age range check\n    if \"age\" in body:\n        age = body[\"age\"]\n        if isinstance(age, str):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age\n                }\n            }\n        if age < 0 or age > 120:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"age must be between 0 and 120\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age,\n                    \"min_value\": 0,\n                    \"max_value\": 120\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Name length check\n    if \"name\" in body:\n        name = body[\"name\"]\n        if isinstance(name, int):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_value\": name\n                }\n            }\n        if len(name) > 100:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"name too long\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"provided_length\": len(name),\n                    \"max_length\": 100\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Date format check\n    if \"date\" in body:\n        date = body[\"date\"]\n        try:\n            from datetime import datetime\n            datetime.fromisoformat(date.replace('Z', '+00:00'))\n        except (ValueError, AttributeError):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid date format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"date\",\n                    \"provided_value\": date,\n                    \"expected_format\": \"ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD)\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Array size check\n    if \"items\" in body:\n        items = body[\"items\"]\n        if isinstance(items, dict):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"array\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"object\"\n                }\n            }\n        if len(items) > 50:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"too many items\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"provided_size\": len(items),\n                    \"max_size\": 50\n                }\n            }\n        if len(items) == 0:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"at least one item required\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"provided_size\": 0,\n                    \"min_size\": 1\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Default: valid request\n    return {\n        \"status\": 201,\n        \"error\": None,\n        \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n    }\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# TEST EXECUTORS\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef validation_test_executor(input_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Mock executor for validation error tests.\n\n    In a real implementation, this would:\n    1. Make an actual HTTP request to the endpoint\n    2. Parse the response\n    3. Extract status, error type, message, and fields\n\n    For testing purposes, this simulates expected responses based on input.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Extract input data\n    body = input_data.get(\"body\", {})\n    endpoint = input_data.get(\"endpoint\", \"\")\n    method = input_data.get(\"method\", \"GET\")\n\n    # Check for validation errors in the input\n    # (In a real implementation, this would come from the actual API response)\n\n    # Happy path tests\n    if input_data.get(\"tags\") and \"happy-path\" in input_data.get(\"tags\", []):\n        return {\n            \"status\": 201,\n            \"error\": None,\n            \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n        }\n\n    # Missing required field checks\n    required_field_checks = {\n        \"/api/users\": [\"name\", \"email\"],\n        \"/api/orders\": [\"customer_id\", \"items\"],\n        \"/api/events\": [\"title\", \"date\"]\n    }\n\n    # Check for missing fields\n    if endpoint in required_field_checks:\n        required_fields = required_field_checks[endpoint]\n        missing_fields = [f for f in required_fields if f not in body or body[f] is None]\n\n        if missing_fields:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": f\"Missing required field(s): {', '.join(missing_fields)}\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"missing_fields\": missing_fields,\n                    \"count\": len(missing_fields)\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Email format check\n    if \"email\" in body:\n        email = body[\"email\"]\n        if \"@\" not in email or \".\" not in email.split(\"@\")[-1]:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid email format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"email\",\n                    \"provided_value\": email,\n                    \"constraint\": \"must contain @ and valid domain\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Age range check\n    if \"age\" in body:\n        age = body[\"age\"]\n        if isinstance(age, str):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age\n                }\n            }\n        if age < 0 or age > 120:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"age must be between 0 and 120\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age,\n                    \"min_value\": 0,\n                    \"max_value\": 120\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Name length check\n    if \"name\" in body:\n        name = body[\"name\"]\n        if isinstance(name, int):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_value\": name\n                }\n            }\n        if len(name) > 100:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"name too long\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"provided_length\": len(name),\n                    \"max_length\": 100\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Date format check\n    if \"date\" in body:\n        date = body[\"date\"]\n        try:\n            from datetime import datetime\n            datetime.fromisoformat(date.replace('Z', '+00:00'))\n        except (ValueError, AttributeError):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid date format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"date\",\n                    \"provided_value\": date,\n                    \"expected_format\": \"ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD)\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Array size check\n    if \"items\" in body:\n        items = body[\"items\"]\n        if isinstance(items, dict):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"array\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"object\"\n                }\n            }\n        if len(items) > 50:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"too many items\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"provided_size\": len(items),\n                    \"max_size\": 50\n                }\n            }\n        if len(items) == 0:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"at least one item required\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"provided_size\": 0,\n                    \"min_size\": 1\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Default: valid request\n    return {\n        \"status\": 201,\n        \"error\": None,\n        \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n    }\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# TEST EXECUTORS\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef validation_test_executor(input_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Mock executor for validation error tests.\n\n    In a real implementation, this would:\n    1. Make an actual HTTP request to the endpoint\n    2. Parse the response\n    3. Extract status, error type, message, and fields\n\n    For testing purposes, this simulates expected responses based on input.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Extract input data\n    body = input_data.get(\"body\", {})\n    endpoint = input_data.get(\"endpoint\", \"\")\n    method = input_data.get(\"method\", \"GET\")\n\n    # Check for validation errors in the input\n    # (In a real implementation, this would come from the actual API response)\n\n    # Happy path tests\n    if input_data.get(\"tags\") and \"happy-path\" in input_data.get(\"tags\", []):\n        return {\n            \"status\": 201,\n            \"error\": None,\n            \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n        }\n\n    # Missing required field checks\n    required_field_checks = {\n        \"/api/users\": [\"name\", \"email\"],\n        \"/api/orders\": [\"customer_id\", \"items\"],\n        \"/api/events\": [\"title\", \"date\"]\n    }\n\n    # Check for missing fields\n    if endpoint in required_field_checks:\n        required_fields = required_field_checks[endpoint]\n        missing_fields = [f for f in required_fields if f not in body or body[f] is None]\n\n        if missing_fields:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": f\"Missing required field(s): {', '.join(missing_fields)}\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"missing_fields\": missing_fields,\n                    \"count\": len(missing_fields)\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Email format check\n    if \"email\" in body:\n        email = body[\"email\"]\n        if \"@\" not in email or \".\" not in email.split(\"@\")[-1]:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid email format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"email\",\n                    \"provided_value\": email,\n                    \"constraint\": \"must contain @ and valid domain\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Age range check\n    if \"age\" in body:\n        age = body[\"age\"]\n        if isinstance(age, str):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age\n                }\n            }\n        if age < 0 or age > 120:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"age must be between 0 and 120\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age,\n                    \"min_value\": 0,\n                    \"max_value\": 120\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Name length check\n    if \"name\" in body:\n        name = body[\"name\"]\n        if isinstance(name, int):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_value\": name\n                }\n            }\n        if len(name) > 100:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"name too long\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"provided_length\": len(name),\n                    \"max_length\": 100\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Date format check\n    if \"date\" in body:\n        date = body[\"date\"]\n        try:\n            from datetime import datetime\n            datetime.fromisoformat(date.replace('Z', '+00:00'))\n        except (ValueError, AttributeError):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid date format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"date\",\n                    \"provided_value\": date,\n                    \"expected_format\": \"ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD)\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Array size check\n    if \"items\" in body:\n        items = body[\"items\"]\n        if isinstance(items, dict):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"array\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"object\"\n                }\n            }\n        if len(items) > 50:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"too many items\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"provided_size\": len(items),\n                    \"max_size\": 50\n                }\n            }\n        if len(items) == 0:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"at least one item required\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"provided_size\": 0,\n                    \"min_size\": 1\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Default: valid request\n    return {\n        \"status\": 201,\n        \"error\": None,\n        \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n    }\n\n\n# create_missing_required_fields_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for missing required field scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"missing-field\")\n\n\ndef create_invalid_format_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for invalid format scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"invalid-format\")\n\n\ndef create_out_of_range_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for out-of-range value scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"out-of-range\")\n\n\ndef create_type_mismatch_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for type mismatch scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"type-mismatch\")\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# TEST EXECUTORS\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef validation_test_executor(input_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Mock executor for validation error tests.\n\n    In a real implementation, this would:\n    1. Make an actual HTTP request to the endpoint\n    2. Parse the response\n    3. Extract status, error type, message, and fields\n\n    For testing purposes, this simulates expected responses based on input.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Extract input data\n    body = input_data.get(\"body\", {})\n    endpoint = input_data.get(\"endpoint\", \"\")\n    method = input_data.get(\"method\", \"GET\")\n\n    # Check for validation errors in the input\n    # (In a real implementation, this would come from the actual API response)\n\n    # Happy path tests\n    if input_data.get(\"tags\") and \"happy-path\" in input_data.get(\"tags\", []):\n        return {\n            \"status\": 201,\n            \"error\": None,\n            \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n        }\n\n    # Missing required field checks\n    required_field_checks = {\n        \"/api/users\": [\"name\", \"email\"],\n        \"/api/orders\": [\"customer_id\", \"items\"],\n        \"/api/events\": [\"title\", \"date\"]\n    }\n\n    # Check for missing fields\n    if endpoint in required_field_checks:\n        required_fields = required_field_checks[endpoint]\n        missing_fields = [f for f in required_fields if f not in body or body[f] is None]\n\n        if missing_fields:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": f\"Missing required field(s): {', '.join(missing_fields)}\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"missing_fields\": missing_fields,\n                    \"count\": len(missing_fields)\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Email format check\n    if \"email\" in body:\n        email = body[\"email\"]\n        if \"@\" not in email or \".\" not in email.split(\"@\")[-1]:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid email format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"email\",\n                    \"provided_value\": email,\n                    \"constraint\": \"must contain @ and valid domain\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Age range check\n    if \"age\" in body:\n        age = body[\"age\"]\n        if isinstance(age, str):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age\n                }\n            }\n        if age < 0 or age > 120:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"age must be between 0 and 120\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age,\n                    \"min_value\": 0,\n                    \"max_value\": 120\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Name length check\n    if \"name\" in body:\n        name = body[\"name\"]\n        if isinstance(name, int):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_value\": name\n                }\n            }\n        if len(name) > 100:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"name too long\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"provided_length\": len(name),\n                    \"max_length\": 100\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Date format check\n    if \"date\" in body:\n        date = body[\"date\"]\n        try:\n            from datetime import datetime\n            datetime.fromisoformat(date.replace('Z', '+00:00'))\n        except (ValueError, AttributeError):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid date format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"date\",\n                    \"provided_value\": date,\n                    \"expected_format\": \"ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD)\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Array size check\n    if \"items\" in body:\n        items = body[\"items\"]\n        if isinstance(items, dict):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"array\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"object\"\n                }\n            }\n        if len(items) > 50:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"too many items\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"provided_size\": len(items),\n                    \"max_size\": 50\n                }\n            }\n        if len(items) == 0:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"at least one item required\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"provided_size\": 0,\n                    \"min_size\": 1\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Default: valid request\n    return {\n        \"status\": 201,\n        \"error\": None,\n        \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n    }\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# TEST EXECUTORS\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef validation_test_executor(input_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Mock executor for validation error tests.\n\n    In a real implementation, this would:\n    1. Make an actual HTTP request to the endpoint\n    2. Parse the response\n    3. Extract status, error type, message, and fields\n\n    For testing purposes, this simulates expected responses based on input.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Extract input data\n    body = input_data.get(\"body\", {})\n    endpoint = input_data.get(\"endpoint\", \"\")\n    method = input_data.get(\"method\", \"GET\")\n\n    # Check for validation errors in the input\n    # (In a real implementation, this would come from the actual API response)\n\n    # Happy path tests\n    if input_data.get(\"tags\") and \"happy-path\" in input_data.get(\"tags\", []):\n        return {\n            \"status\": 201,\n            \"error\": None,\n            \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n        }\n\n    # Missing required field checks\n    required_field_checks = {\n in required_field_checks:\n        required_fields = required_field_checks[endpoint]\n        missing_fields = [f for f in required_fields if f not in body or body[f] is None]\n\n        if missing_fields:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": f\"Missing required field(s): {', '.join(missing_fields)}\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"missing_fields\": missing_fields,\n                    \"count\": len(missing_fields)\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Email format check\n    if \"email\" in body:\n        email = body[\"email\"]\n        if \"@\" not in email or \".\" not in email.split(\"@\")[-1]:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid email format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"email\",\n                    \"provided_value\": email,\n                    \"constraint\": \"must contain @ and valid domain\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Age range check\n    if \"age\" in body:\n        age = body[\"age\"]\n        if isinstance(age, str):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age\n                }\n            }\n        if age < 0 or age > 120:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"age must be between 0 and 120\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age,\n                    \"min_value\": 0,\n                    \"max_value\": 120\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Name length check\n    if \"name\" in body:\n        name = body[\"name\"]\n        if isinstance(name, int):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_value\": name\n                }\n            }\n        if len(name) > 100:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"name too long\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"provided_length\": len(name),\n                    \"max_length\": 100\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Date format check\n    if \"date\" in body:\n        date = body[\"date\"]\n        try:\n            from datetime \"provided_value\": date,\n                    \"expected_format\": \"ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD)\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Array size check\n    if \"items\" in body:\n        items = body[\"items\"]\n        if isinstance(items, dict):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"array\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"object\"\n                }\n            }\n        if len(items) > 50:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"too many =============================================================================\n# TEST EXECUTORS\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef validation_test_executor(input_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Mock executor for validation error tests.\n\n    In a real implementation, this would:\n    1. Make an actual HTTP request to the endpoint\n    2. Parse the response\n    3. Extract status, error type, message, and fields\n\n    For testing purposes, this simulates expected responses based on input.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Extract input data\n    body = input_data.get(\"body\", {})\n    endpoint = input_data.get(\"endpoint\", \"\")\n    method = input_data.get(\"method\", \"GET\")\n\n    # Check for validation errors in the input\n    # (In a real implementation, this would come from the actual API response)\n\n    # Happy path tests\n    if input_data.get(\"tags\") and \"happy-path\" in input_data.get(\"tags\", []):\n        return {\n            \"status\": 201,\n            \"error\": None,\n            \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n        }\n\n    # Missing required field checks\n    required_field_checks = {\n in required_field_checks:\n        required_fields = required_field_checks[endpoint]\n        missing_fields = [f for f in required_fields if f not in body or body[f] is None]\n\n        if missing_fields:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": f\"Missing required field(s): {', '.join(missing_fields)}\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"missing_fields\": missing_fields,\n                    \"count\": len(missing_fields)\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Email format check\n    if \"email\" in body:\n        email = body[\"email\"]\n        if \"@\" not in email or \".\" not in email.split(\"@\")[-1]:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid email format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"email\",\n                    \"provided_value\": email,\n                    \"constraint\": \"must contain @ and valid domain\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Age range check\n    if \"age\" in body:\n        age = body[\"age\"]\n        if isinstance(age, str):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age\n                }\n            }\n        if age < 0 or age > 120:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"age must be between 0 and 120\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age,\n                    \"min_value\": 0,\n                    \"max_value\": 120\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Name length check\n    if \"name\" in body:\n        name = body[\"name\"]\n        if isinstance(name, int):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type          \"provided_value\": name\n                }\n            }\n        if len(name) > 100:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"name too long\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"provided_length\": len(name),\n                    \"max_length\": 100\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Date format check\n    if \"date\" in body:\n        date = body[\"date\"]\n        try:\n            from datetime create_missing_required_fields_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for missing required field scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"missing-field\")\n\n\ndef create_invalid_format_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for invalid format test table for type mismatch scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"type-mismatch\")\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# TEST EXECUTORS\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef validation_test_executor(input_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Mock executor for validation error tests.\n\n    In a real implementation, this would:\n    1. Make an actual HTTP request to the endpoint\n    2. Parse the response\n    3. Extract status, error type, message, and fields\n\n    For testing purposes, this simulates expected responses based on input.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Extract input data\n    body = input_data.get(\"body\", {})\n    endpoint = input_data.get(\"endpoint\", \"\")\n    method = input_data.get(\"method\", \"GET\")\n\n    # Check for validation errors in the input\n    # (In a real implementation, this would come from the actual API response)\n\n    # Happy path tests\n    if input_data.get(\"tags\") and \"happy-path\" in input_data.get(\"tags\", []):\n        return {\n            \"status\": 201,\n            \"error\": None,\n            \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n        }\n\n    # Missing required field checks\n    required_field_checks = {\n        \"/api/users\": [\"name\", \"email\"],\n        \"/api/orders\": [\"customer_id\", \"items\"],\n        \"/api/events\": [\"title\", \"date\"]\n    }\n\n    # Check for missing fields\n    if endpoint in required_field_checks:\n        required_fields = required_field_checks[endpoint]\n        missing_fields = [f for f in required_fields if f not in body or body[f] is None]\n\n        if missing_fields:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": f\"Missing required field(s): {', '.join(missing_fields)}\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"missing_fields\": missing_fields,\n                    \"count\": len(missing_fields)\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Email format check\n    if \"email\" in body:\n        email = body[\"email\"]\n        if \"@\" not in email or \".\" not in email.split(\"@\")[-1]:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid email format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"email\",\n                    \"provided_value\": email,\n                    \"constraint\": \"must contain @ and valid domain\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Age range check\n    if \"age\" in body:\n        age = body[\"age\"]\n        if isinstance(age, str):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age\n                }\n            }\n        if age < 0 or age > 120:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"age must be between 0 and 120\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age,\n                    \"min_value\": 0,\n                    \"max_value\": 120\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Name length check\n    if \"name\" in body:\n        name = body[\"name\"]\n        if isinstance(name, int):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_value\": name\n                }\n            }\n        if len(name) > 100:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"name too long\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"name\",\n                    \"provided_length\": len(name),\n                    \"max_length\": 100\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Date format check\n    if \"date\" in body:\n        date = body[\"date\"]\n        try:\n            from datetime import datetime\n            datetime.fromisoformat(date.replace('Z', '+00:00'))\n        except (ValueError, AttributeError):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid date format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"date\",\n                    \"provided_value\": date,\n                    \"expected_format\": \"ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD)\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Array size check\n    if \"items\" in body:\n        items = body[\"items\"]\n        if isinstance(items, dict):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"array\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"object\"\n                }\n            }\n        if len(items) > 50:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"too many items\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"provided_size\": len(items),\n                    \"max_size\": 50\n                }\n            }\n        if len(items) == 0:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"at least one item required\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"items\",\n                    \"provided_size\": 0,\n                    \"min_size\": 1\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Default: valid request\n    return {\n        \"status\": 201,\n        \"error\": None,\n        \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n    }\n\n\n# define test classes only if pytest is available\nif PYTEST_AVAILABLE:\n    class TestMissingRequiredFields:\n        \"\"\"Test cases for missing required field validation.\"\"\"\n\n        def {results.execution_time_ms:.2f}ms\")\n\n            # Show failed tests if any\n            if results.failed_count > 0:\n                print(f\"\\nFailed Tests:\")\n                for result in {result.error_message}\")\n\n            assert results.all_passed, f\"Some tests failed: {results.failed_count} failures\"\n\n        def test_validation_test_table_structure(self):\n            \"\"\"Verify the validation test table has proper structure.\"\"\"\n            table = create_comprehensive_validation_test_table()\n\n            # Check table properties\n            assert table.name == \"comprehensive_validation_errors\"\n            assert table.description is not None\n            assert len(table.test_cases) > 0\n\n            # Check that we have tests for all required categories\n            tags = set()\n            for test_case in table.test_cases:\n                tags.update(test_case.tags)\n\n            required_categories = [\"missing-field\", =============================================================================\n# TEST EXECUTORS\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef validation_test_executor(input_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Mock executor for validation error tests.\n\n    In a real implementation, this would:\n    1. Make an actual HTTP request to the endpoint\n    2. Parse the response\n    3. Extract status, error type, message, and fields\n\n    For testing purposes, this simulates expected responses based on input.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Extract input data\n    body = input_data.get(\"body\", {})\n    endpoint = input_data.get(\"endpoint\", \"\")\n    method = input_data.get(\"method\", \"GET\")\n\n    # Check for validation errors in the input\n    # (In a real implementation, this would come from the actual API response)\n\n    # Happy path tests\n    if input_data.get(\"tags\") and \"happy-path\" in input_data.get(\"tags\", []):\n        return {\n            \"status\": 201,\n            \"error\": None,\n            \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n        }\n\n    # Missing required field checks\n    required_field_checks = {\n        \"/api/users\": [\"name\", \"email\"],\n        \"/api/orders\": [\"customer_id\", \"items\"],\n        \"/api/events\": [\"title\", \"date\"]\n    }\n\n    # Check for missing fields\n    if endpoint in required_field_checks:\n        required_fields = required_field_checks[endpoint]\n        missing_fields = [f for f in required_fields if f not in body or body[f] is None]\n\n        if missing_fields:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": f\"Missing required field(s): {', '.join(missing_fields)}\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"missing_fields\": missing_fields,\n                    \"count\": len(missing_fields)\n                }\n create_missing_required_fields_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for missing required field scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"missing-field\")\n\n\ndef create_invalid_format_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for invalid format scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"invalid-format\")\n\n\ndef create_out_of_range_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for out-of-range value scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"out-of-range\")\n\n\ndef create_type_mismatch_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n    \"\"\"Create test table for type mismatch scenarios.\"\"\"\n    return create_comprehensive_validation_test_table().filter_by_tags(\"type-mismatch\")\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# TEST EXECUTORS\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef validation_test_executor(input_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Mock executor for validation error tests.\n\n    In a real implementation, this would:\n    1. Make an actual HTTP request to the endpoint\n    2. Parse the response\n    3. Extract status, error type, message, and fields\n\n    For testing purposes, this simulates expected responses based on input.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Extract input data\n    body = input_data.get(\"body\", {})\n    endpoint = input_data.get(\"endpoint\", \"\")\n    method = input_data.get(\"method\", \"GET\")\n\n    # Check for validation errors in the input\n    # (In a real implementation, this would come from the actual API response)\n\n    # Happy path tests\n    if input_data.get(\"tags\") and \"happy-path\" in input_data.get(\"tags\", []):\n        return {\n            \"status\": 201,\n            \"error\": None,\n            \"message\": \"Resource created successfully\"\n        }\n\n    # Missing required field checks\n    required_field_checks = {\n        \"/api/users\": [\"name\", \"email\"],\n        \"/api/orders\": [\"customer_id\", \"items\"],\n        \"/api/events\": [\"title\", \"date\"]\n    }\n\n    # Check for missing fields\n    if endpoint in required_field_checks:\n        required_fields = required_field_checks[endpoint]\n        missing_fields = [f for f in required_fields if f not in body or body[f] is None]\n\n        if missing_fields:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": f\"Missing required field(s): {', '.join(missing_fields)}\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"missing_fields\": missing_fields,\n                    \"count\": len(missing_fields)\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Email format check\n    if \"email\" in body:\n        email = body[\"email\"]\n        if \"@\" not in email or \".\" not in email.split(\"@\")[-1]:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"Invalid email format\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"email\",\n                    \"provided_value\": email,\n                    \"constraint\": \"must contain @ and valid domain\"\n                }\n            }\n\n    # Age range check\n    if \"age\" in body:\n        age = body[\"age\"]\n        if isinstance(age, str):\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"type mismatch\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n                    \"expected_type\": \"number\",\n                    \"provided_type\": \"string\",\n                    \"provided_value\": age\n                }\n            }\n        if age < 0 or age > 120:\n            return {\n                \"status\": 422,\n                \"error\": \"validation_error\",\n                \"message\": \"age must be between 0 and 120\",\n                \"fields\": {\n                    \"field\": \"age\",\n
